sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Ad-Tech Company AdTheorent Reaches $1 Billion SPAC Deal to Go Public
feed.summary: Ad-Tech Company AdTheorent is nearing a SPAC deal valued at approximately $1 billion to go public. The firm, which uses machine learning to optimize advertising campaigns without relying on sensitive personal data, joins other digital advertising companies in leveraging SPACs for public listing. This move allows AdTheorent to bypass the traditional IPO process.

feed.title: India's e-commerce sector sees big growth in 2021
feed.summary: India's online retail sector experienced only 7-8% growth in 2020, significantly less than the 20% seen in the US and China, due to lockdown impacts and an economic slowdown. However, major players like Amazon and Flipkart anticipate strong sales growth in 2021, driven by new online shoppers and increased spending in sectors like online grocery and e-pharmacy. The industry also expects continued consolidation and momentum in investments for social commerce and live streaming.

feed.title: Playa Vista Amazon, Chick-Fil-A Get $168 Million Loan
feed.summary: A Playa Vista Amazon.com Inc. facility and a Chick-Fil-A location have secured a $168 million permanent refinance loan from Citi, following a previous $110 million bridge refinancing. George Smith Partners’ Antonio Hachem facilitated both financings for the property, which spans 12.5 acres and was previously an auto dealership mall. This transaction highlights Amazon's ongoing real estate expansion, including another recent acquisition in Torrance.

feed.title: Google employees form union in rare move for a US tech giant
feed.summary: Employees of Google and its parent company, Alphabet, have formed a new union called the Alphabet Workers Union, in a rare move for a major US technology company. The union, supported by the Communications Workers of America (CWA), aims to address issues like compensation, employee classification, and the types of work Google undertakes. This initiative follows years of workplace confrontations and dismissals of employee concerns by executives.

feed.title: Top Tips and Tricks for Amazon 3rd Party Sellers
feed.summary: This article provides ten essential tips for Amazon third-party sellers looking to boost sales and maintain momentum on the platform. Key advice includes diversifying marketing efforts beyond Amazon, strategically choosing product niches, understanding customer needs, and focusing on excellent customer service and seller ratings. It also emphasizes the importance of managing inventory, actively seeking reviews, and being open to alternative selling platforms if Amazon doesn't yield desired results.

feed.title: Closely watched healthcare play by Amazon, JPMorgan and Berkshire Hathaway to shutter
feed.summary: Haven, the ambitious healthcare venture created by Amazon, JPMorgan Chase, and Berkshire Hathaway, is set to shut down by the end of February. The company, which aimed to tackle healthcare complexity and costs, had garnered significant attention but also saw executive departures and few public achievements. Many of its 60 employees are expected to be absorbed by the founding companies, which will continue their individual healthcare initiatives.

feed.title: Discovery Plus Launches on Multiple Platforms, Including Roku and Amazon Fire TV
feed.summary: Discovery Plus has officially launched in the U.S. and is now available on a wide array of platforms, including Roku, Amazon Fire TV, Apple devices, Google devices, Microsoft Xbox, and Samsung Smart TVs. This broad availability is part of Discovery's strategy to make its real-life entertainment content accessible everywhere, leveraging strong partnerships with leading distributors. The launch follows deals with Vodafone, Verizon, and Sky, expanding its reach across the U.S., U.K., and European territories.

feed.title: Our Beauty Director Shares Her Tips for Combatting Winter Skin
feed.summary: Women’s Health Beauty Director Kristina Rodulfo provides expert tips and product recommendations from Ulta Beauty to combat common winter skin issues like dryness, redness, and flaking. Her advice includes double-cleansing, using hydrating mists, incorporating niacinamide serums for skin barrier support, and layering rich moisturizers. She also emphasizes the importance of eye cream for the delicate skin around the eyes.

feed.title: SpaceX, L3Harris pursue hypersonic missile defense system
feed.summary: SpaceX and L3Harris Technologies have secured contracts to develop a space-based defense network aimed at countering hypersonic missiles. This initiative, driven by escalating threats from Russia and China, involves the rapid deployment of numerous satellites for detection and communication. The project emphasizes collaboration among competing firms and a more efficient, high-volume approach to satellite production.

feed.title: Why Apple became a $2 trillion company in 2020, and what could come next
feed.summary: Apple's market valuation soared to over $2 trillion in 2020, largely due to a significant re-rating of its services business by investors, which now accounts for roughly half of its total value. While the iPhone remains crucial, the company faces challenges with its streaming content, risks to its Google search licensing revenue, and an undefined strategy for the EV market. Analysts are optimistic about continued growth, but some caution about the stock's valuation, given the service's growth rate and potential threats to key revenue streams like the Google licensing deal and App Store commissions.

feed.title: Haven, the Amazon-Berkshire-JPMorgan venture to disrupt health care, is disbanding after 3 years
feed.summary: Haven, the joint venture formed by Amazon, Berkshire Hathaway, and JPMorgan Chase to revolutionize healthcare, is disbanding after three years. Many of its 57 employees are expected to be placed within the founding companies, which will continue to pursue individual healthcare initiatives and collaborate informally. The closure highlights the significant challenges of disrupting the complex U.S. healthcare system.

feed.title: Low Inflation? Asset Prices at Record Highs Suggests Otherwise
feed.summary: Frank Holmes argues that official low inflation figures do not reflect the reality of soaring asset and commodity prices. He highlights significant increases in gold, Bitcoin, food, lumber, and copper prices, attributing these rises to massive government stimulus and easy monetary policy. Holmes suggests that investors should be critical of media biases and consider holding hard assets as stores of value given concerns about currency debasement.

feed.title: Amazon, Berkshire and JPMorgan Will End Joint Health Care Venture (Published 2021)
feed.summary: The joint venture Haven, formed by Amazon, Berkshire Hathaway, and JPMorgan Chase to innovate health care delivery for their employees, is disbanding at the end of February, three years after its inception. The initiative, which aimed to disrupt the health care industry by leveraging the scale of three large employers, faced logistical hurdles in developing solutions applicable to all three companies. This quiet dissolution marks the end of a high-profile collaboration that once sent shock waves through the health insurance sector.

feed.title: Gyms aren’t coming back. Here’s how you’ll work out in the future
feed.summary: The COVID-19 pandemic has accelerated a shift towards at-home fitness, with a significant percentage of Americans not planning to return to physical gyms. Companies like Peloton have seen massive growth, and new business models are emerging where instructors have more autonomy, and gyms may need to rebrand as social clubs to stay relevant. The future of fitness is expected to be a hybrid approach, combining home workouts, outdoor activities, and potentially social-focused gym experiences.

feed.title: C by GE gets rebranded under new ownership, expands beyond lighting
feed.summary: GE Lighting, acquired by Savant, is rebranding its "C by GE" smart home line to "Cync" and expanding beyond just lighting products. The new brand will introduce a thermostat, outdoor smart plug, connected camera, and fan speed switch at CES, along with a new Cync app in March. This strategic move aims to position GE Lighting as a broader smart-home entity in an increasingly competitive market.

feed.title: Jeff Bezos, Jamie Dimon and Warren Buffett tried to solve health care. 3 years later, their company has shut down
feed.summary: Haven, the joint healthcare venture founded by Amazon, Berkshire Hathaway, and JPMorgan Chase, is shutting down after three years. The company aimed to provide better healthcare services at lower costs for its employees but struggled to expand beyond its founding partners. The three companies will continue to collaborate informally to address their individual employee healthcare needs.

feed.title: Amazon buys 11 aircraft to expand air cargo fleet, four of them from WestJet
feed.summary: Amazon.com Inc. is purchasing 11 used Boeing 767-300 planes, marking its first aircraft acquisition instead of leasing to expand its air cargo operations. Four of these planes are from WestJet Airlines Ltd. and seven from Delta Air Lines Inc., significantly growing Amazon's fleet to over 85 planes by the end of next year. This move intensifies Amazon's competition with traditional cargo carriers like FedEx Corp. and UPS Inc., leveraging the depressed aircraft market due to the COVID-19 pandemic.

feed.title: Cable One Preps IP-Based Sparklight TV
feed.summary: Sparklight, a subsidiary of Cable One, is set to launch Sparklight TV, an IP-based TV service, in early 2021. This transition to IPTV aims to reclaim spectrum, freeing up network capacity to enhance Gigabit offerings and establish a 10G technology platform. The service will be accessible via streaming apps on platforms like Amazon Fire TV, Android TV, and Apple TV.

feed.title: Hundreds of Best Buy Stores will have E INK Price Labels
feed.summary: Best Buy has invested $13 million to install E INK-based Electronic Shelf Labels (ESL) in 119 more U.S. stores by the end of 2021. This move follows previous installations in nearly 680 stores, indicating a large-scale shift from paper to digital price tags. The ESL technology, supplied by Swedish company Pricer, allows for quick price updates and QR code changes, significantly improving efficiency for retailers.

feed.title: Amazon Launches $2 Billion Housing Equity Fund
feed.summary: Amazon has launched a $2 billion Housing Equity Fund dedicated to preserving and creating over 20,000 affordable housing units in the Puget Sound region, Arlington, Virginia, and Nashville, Tennessee. The fund will provide below-market loans and grants to various housing partners, targeting households earning between 30% and 80% of the area median income. Initial investments include projects in Arlington and Washington state, aiming to ensure long-term affordability and support inclusive community development.

feed.title: Amazon provides bulk of financing for massive Arlington housing acquisition through new housing fund
feed.summary: Amazon has committed hundreds of millions of dollars in below-market loans and grants to support the Washington Housing Conservancy's purchase of the Crystal House apartments in Arlington. This significant financial backing is part of Amazon's broader efforts in affordable housing, mirroring initiatives in Washington state and Tennessee. The investment underlines Amazon's increasing role in addressing housing affordability issues in areas impacted by its growth.

feed.title: Interested In Apple But Not Its Volatility? 2 ETFs To Preserve Stability
feed.summary: This article suggests two ETFs for investors interested in Apple's potential returns but wary of its volatility. It highlights the iShares Russell Top 200 Growth ETF (IWY) and the Fidelity MSCI Information Technology Index ETF (FTEC) as ways to gain exposure to Apple and other growth/tech companies while potentially mitigating short-term price swings. The article details the holdings, performance, and financial metrics of both ETFs.

feed.title: Crystal House apartments near Amazon HQ2 acquired by affordable housing nonprofit
feed.summary: The Washington Housing Conservancy has acquired the Crystal House apartments near Amazon.com Inc.'s second headquarters, with JBG Smith tapped to manage the property. The acquisition aims to stabilize rents in the area, as housing costs are expected to rise due to the proximity to Amazon HQ2.

feed.title: There were plenty of red flags that spelled the demise of Amazon, JPMorgan healthcare venture, experts say
feed.summary: Experts suggest that Haven, the healthcare venture by Amazon, JPMorgan Chase, and Berkshire Hathaway, faced numerous challenges from its inception, leading to its eventual shutdown. Problems included a lack of strategic clarity, executive turnover, forming as a nonprofit, and internal issues complicated by competing interests of its parent companies. The failure highlights the complexity of disrupting the American healthcare business model and suggests that future efforts need to leverage specialized healthcare expertise.

feed.title: Elon Musk overtakes Bezos to become world’s richest person
feed.summary: Elon Musk, CEO of Tesla and SpaceX, has surpassed Amazon's Jeff Bezos to become the world's richest person. His net worth reached $188.5 billion after a 4.8% rally in Tesla's shares on Thursday, just over $1.5 billion more than Bezos. This milestone caps a remarkable year for Musk, whose net worth soared by over $150 billion, largely due to Tesla's impressive 743% stock surge in the past year.

feed.title: NASA's HiRise Camera Captures Stunning New Images of Valles Marineris, the Grand Canyon of Mars
feed.summary: NASA's HiRise camera on the Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter has captured new stunning images of Valles Marineris, the largest known canyon in the Solar System. This Martian canyon is significantly larger than Earth's Grand Canyon, extending over 3,000 kilometers long and 8 kilometers deep. The HiRise camera, which has been studying Mars since 2006, provides incredibly high-resolution images, aiding scientists in understanding the planet's geological formations.

feed.title: Big Oil Big Tech Big Data
feed.summary: The oil and gas (O&G) industry, facing instability and a lag in digital transformation, is increasingly collaborating with big tech companies to leverage cloud computing, AI, and big data. These partnerships aim to cut costs, enhance operational efficiency, and improve safety and environmental performance through solutions like predictive maintenance, digital twins, and remote operations. The digital shift helps O&G companies manage vast amounts of data, reduce unplanned downtime, and potentially save billions in operational expenses.

feed.title: Why the Amazon, JPMorgan, Berkshire Venture Collapsed: ‘Health Care Was Too Big a Problem’
feed.summary: The Haven joint venture, formed by Amazon, JPMorgan Chase, and Berkshire Hathaway to revolutionize healthcare and reduce costs for their employees, is ceasing operations after three years. Despite investing roughly $100 million, the initiative struggled with significant challenges including data sharing, bureaucracy, staff turnover, unclear goals, and unexpected competition. These obstacles prevented Haven from achieving its primary objective of understanding and reducing healthcare costs, leading to its collapse.

feed.title: Livestream shopping is the next big thing in retail
feed.summary: Livestream shopping, initially popularized in China, is rapidly gaining traction globally, fueled by the pandemic and evolving consumer behavior. Mike George, President and CEO of Qurate Retail and Chairman of the National Retail Federation, highlights how retailers are adopting this interactive video approach to engage customers, building on Qurate's nearly 40 years of experience with live video shopping. While offering dazzling possibilities like AI-curated content and virtual reality, success hinges on building lasting customer relationships and competitive economics rather than just technological flash.

feed.title: Fintech start-up SoFi to go public via SPAC backed by Chamath Palihapitiya
feed.summary: Fintech startup SoFi announced it will go public by merging with a special purpose acquisition company (SPAC) backed by venture capital investor Chamath Palihapitiya, valuing SoFi at $8.65 billion. SoFi, previously valued at $5.7 billion in private markets, offers various financial services and was chosen by Palihapitiya for its mobile-first approach and potential to disrupt banking. CEO Anthony Noto cited "deal certainty" as a key reason for choosing the SPAC route over a traditional IPO.

feed.title: Online Entertainment Market Expected to Witness High Growth
feed.summary: The online entertainment market, valued at $183,067.0 million in 2019, is projected to reach $652,455.0 million by 2027, growing at a CAGR of 20.82% from 2021 to 2027. This growth is driven by increased competition and strategic partnerships, with key players including Netflix, Google LLC, Facebook, Tencent Holdings Ltd, and Sony Corp. The report provides detailed segmentation, competitive analysis, and regional estimates for the market.

feed.title: Rackspace 2021: A newly publicly traded company begins new year with fresh outlook - San Antonio Business Journal
feed.summary: Rackspace begins 2021 as a newly public company, having gone public in August 2020. Despite initial stock fluctuations, analysts remain optimistic about the company's prospects. The article highlights recent partnerships and financial reports.

feed.title: Survey: 29% of people would rather quit than return to the workplace
feed.summary: A survey by LiveCareer indicates that 29% of individuals would prefer to quit their jobs rather than return to a physical workplace, with over 60% desiring to continue working from home post-pandemic. While companies are preparing for employees to return, with some making it mandatory, experts suggest that flexibility and compromise, like hybrid models, might be offered. Employees hesitant to return are advised to communicate with their managers, especially if health is a concern, and be prepared to discuss flexible arrangements.

feed.title: Avison Young negotiates sale of 1.75-MSF national truck terminal portfolio
feed.summary: Avison Young has successfully negotiated the sale of a 1.75-million-square-foot national truck terminal portfolio on behalf of CenterPoint Properties to institutional investors advised by J.P. Morgan Asset Management. The portfolio comprises approximately 53 truck terminals in key logistics markets across the U.S., including major hubs like Chicago and Dallas. This acquisition aims to capitalize on the growth of e-commerce by providing critical logistics infrastructure, with many properties leased to top-tier logistics tenants.

feed.title: Tesla market value crosses $800 billion for the first time
feed.summary: Tesla's market capitalization surpassed $800 billion for the first time, driven by a 5.6% jump in its shares. This surge also propelled CEO Elon Musk past Jeff Bezos to become the world's wealthiest individual. The electric automaker's valuation now dwarfs the combined market caps of traditional Detroit automakers, despite its production being a fraction of larger rivals.

feed.title: Tesla market value crosses $800 billion for the first time
feed.summary: Tesla Inc.'s market capitalization surpassed $800 billion for the first time, driven by a significant jump in its stock price on Friday. This surge has also propelled CEO Elon Musk to become the world's richest man, surpassing Amazon's Jeff Bezos. The company's valuation now places it among Wall Street's most valuable firms, despite its production volume being a fraction of larger automotive rivals.

feed.title: These Hot Stocks Made This American Century's Best 2020 U.S. Diversified Stock Fund
feed.summary: The American Century Focused Dynamic Growth Fund (ACFSX) was the top-performing U.S. diversified stock fund in 2020, up 73.63% compared to the S&P 500's 18.40%. The fund achieved this success by investing in high-growth companies like Amazon, Facebook, Alphabet, Tesla, Square, DocuSign, Nike, Chipotle Mexican Grill, and Chegg, focusing on those with high sustainable growth rates and strong fundamentals, consistent with CAN SLIM principles. Its strong performance has earned it repeated IBD Best Mutual Funds Awards.

feed.title: Direxion Debuts 2X Leveraged Cloud Computing ETFs
feed.summary: Direxion has launched two new 2X leveraged ETFs, CLDL and CLDS, focusing on companies involved in cloud computing services. These ETFs provide 200% or 200% inverse daily performance of the Indxx USA Cloud Computing Index, catering to traders looking for leveraged exposure in this high-growth sector. The company emphasizes these products are for investors who understand the risks of leveraged investments and actively monitor their positions.

feed.title: Will Commodities Ride the "Blue Wave" Higher in 2021?
feed.summary: This article discusses the potential impact of a Democratic-controlled Congress and White House on commodities markets in 2021, particularly focusing on green energy initiatives and increased government spending. It highlights silver and copper as top-performing commodities and examines the drivers behind their price appreciation, along with the performance of gold, Bitcoin, and various sectors. The author also touches on the ongoing challenges posed by the COVID-19 pandemic and geopolitical factors.

feed.title: Low on Workers, Manufacturers Recruit Their Executives for the Factory Floor
feed.summary: Facing severe labor shortages due to Covid-19, child care issues, and competition from e-commerce, American manufacturers are resorting to an unusual solution: deploying their executives and retail employees to work shifts on the factory floor. This strategy is being adopted by companies like Yankee Candle, where R&D managers are packaging products to fulfill orders, as traditional incentives like higher pay are proving insufficient to attract enough workers, leading to choked supply chains and delivery delays.

feed.title: Development Site Near Culver City Sells for $92 Million
feed.summary: A 3.5-acre mixed-use development site near Culver City was sold by Public Storage for $92 million to Australia-based companies Lendlease Corp. and Aware Super. The site is intended for a 500,000-square-foot project, including multifamily units, creative office space, and retail, with an estimated value of $600 million upon completion. The location benefits from its proximity to major tech and entertainment companies and its designation as an opportunity zone provides tax benefits.

feed.title: Amazon, Walmart Tell Consumers to Skip Returns of Unwanted Items
feed.summary: Amazon, Walmart, and other retailers are increasingly telling customers to keep unwanted items rather than returning them. This new practice is driven by AI analysis, which determines that for certain inexpensive or bulky items, refunding the purchase price is more cost-effective than processing a return due to associated shipping and handling costs. This trend highlights a shift in retail return policies aimed at optimizing operational efficiency.

feed.title: Amazon, Walmart using AI to decide on economic sense to process a return
feed.summary: Amazon, Walmart, and other retailers are increasingly using artificial intelligence to determine if it is more cost-effective to refund customers for returned items and let them keep the product, especially for inexpensive or bulky goods. This trend is accelerating due to the surge in online shopping during the COVID-19 pandemic, as companies seek to optimize their returns process. The approach saves retailers money on shipping and processing, while also improving customer satisfaction by simplifying the return experience.

feed.title: Former Amazon VP joins Chewy leadership team
feed.summary: Mark Eamer, former vice president of advertising, product, and media at Amazon, has been appointed chief marketing officer at Chewy, Inc. In this role, Eamer will be responsible for expanding Chewy's marketing and customer engagement initiatives, leveraging his extensive experience in digital platforms and customer experience. Chewy's CEO, Sumit Singh, highlighted Eamer's track record as a builder, innovator, and connector as instrumental in advancing the company's marketing capabilities.

feed.title: Amazon plans to add 5 sites, create 2,000 jobs in Metro Detroit
feed.summary: Amazon announced plans to significantly expand its presence in Metro Detroit with five new sites, expected to create over 2,000 jobs. These sites include fulfillment centers in Detroit, Hazel Park, Pontiac, and Huron Township, plus a sortation center in Plymouth. The company stated these will be full- and part-time positions offering a minimum wage of $15 per hour along with comprehensive benefits.

feed.title: Covid-19 Propels Amazon to Top U.S. Grocery Retailer for the 2021 dunnhumby Retailer Preference Index
feed.summary: Amazon has been named the top U.S. grocery retailer in dunnhumby's fourth annual Retailer Preference Index for 2021, surpassing H-E-B and Trader Joe's. This shift is largely attributed to Covid-19 driven momentum, with Amazon excelling in speed, digital offerings, and customer safety. The study emphasizes that while traditional factors like price and quality remain crucial for long-term growth, the ability to deliver on speed and digital experience became paramount during the pandemic.

feed.title: Staples tries to buy Office Depot for the third time
feed.summary: Staples has made a third attempt to acquire Office Depot, offering $2.1 billion for the office supplies retailer. Previous attempts in 1996 and 2015 were blocked by federal antitrust regulators. This time, Staples aims to address antitrust concerns by proposing to sell Office Depot's commercial business concurrently with the takeover.

feed.title: Amazon.com suspends donations to lawmakers who voted against Biden certification
feed.summary: Amazon.com Inc announced that its political action committee (PAC) would suspend contributions to U.S. lawmakers who voted against the certification of President-elect Joe Biden's election results. This decision was made due to the "unacceptable attempt to undermine a legitimate democratic process." An Amazon spokeswoman confirmed the suspension of contributions to any member of Congress who voted to override the election results.

feed.title: Ballard shares surge after train deal amid hydrogen industry rally
feed.summary: Ballard Power Systems Inc. shares sharply increased after announcing a deal to supply fuel cell modules for a hydrogen-powered train demonstration project in Scotland. This surge is part of a broader rally in the hydrogen industry, fueled by recent announcements from companies like Plug Power Inc. and FuelCell Energy Inc., signaling significant momentum in the alternative energy sector. Analysts view Ballard's participation in the train project as a positive indicator for the nascent hydrogen-powered train market and the expanding opportunities in commercial transportation for fuel cell technology.

feed.title: Comcast yanks political contributions to Republican objectors, citing Capitol riot
feed.summary: Comcast has announced it is suspending political contributions to the 147 Republican officials who voted against certifying the Electoral College results, citing the violence at the U.S. Capitol. This decision aligns Comcast with other major companies like Amazon and Goldman Sachs that are also halting donations to these specific objectors, prompting a review of the company's political giving policies. While some political experts are skeptical of the long-term impact of these suspensions, the action marks a significant response from corporate America to the events following the 2020 election.

feed.title: From brick & mortar to ecommerce in a flash
feed.summary: This article details how PVH Corp., owner of brands like Calvin Klein and Tommy Hilfiger, successfully transitioned its business model from brick-and-mortar to e-commerce during the COVID-19 pandemic. By leveraging prior investments in Cisco technology, including networking, collaboration tools, and security, PVH was able to quickly adapt to remote work and a surge in online demand, even breaking previous Black Friday sales records in April 2020. The experience transformed their operating model, demonstrating the critical role of robust technology and security in enabling agility and collaboration.

feed.title: General Motors unveils EV van as part of new commercial business unit
feed.summary: General Motors announced a new commercial business unit called BrightDrop, which will launch an all-electric van, the EV600, by the end of 2021. The EV600 will be capable of up to 250 miles per charge, and FedEx will be the first customer, receiving the initial 500 vehicles. This initiative is part of GM's $27 billion investment in electric and autonomous vehicles by 2025, strategically targeting the growing commercial EV market.

feed.title: Shopping returns top $428B as Amazon, Walmart say keep it
feed.summary: In 2020, consumers returned $428 billion worth of merchandise, representing 10.6% of total U.S. retail sales, with online returns significantly increasing due to the pandemic. Major retailers like Amazon and Walmart are now using AI to determine if it's more cost-effective to issue refunds and let customers keep items, especially for inexpensive or bulky products, rather than processing returns. This strategy aims to reduce costs associated with shipping and handling returns, which includes an estimated $5.90 loss to fraud for every $100 in returned merchandise.

feed.title: Synchrony CEO Margaret Keane stepping down after nearly a decade leading the co-branded card lender
feed.summary: Synchrony CEO Margaret Keane is stepping down after nearly a decade, with company president Brian Doubles set to take over. Keane, who led Synchrony's separation from GE and oversaw key partnerships with Amazon and Venmo, will remain as executive chair. Doubles will focus on guiding the company through the pandemic and an accelerated shift to an online economy.

feed.title: Spotify Bets Big on Podcasts as a Path to Profitability
feed.summary: Spotify is facing profitability challenges due to its business model, which dedicates over 70% of its revenue to rights holders, preventing it from making a profit since its inception despite high sales. CEO Daniel Ek believes that podcasts, exemplified by investments in major creators like Joe Rogan, offer a crucial path to profitability by diversifying content beyond music and shifting away from reliance on traditional record labels. This strategy aims to give Spotify more control over content costs and attract more users, thereby securing its financial future.

feed.title: Capital One closes its data centres and goes all in with AWS
feed.summary: Capital One has completed a multi-year transformation, exiting all of its data centers and migrating all applications and systems to the public cloud with AWS. This significant change for a financial institution involved redefining its operating model to become a technology company that does banking, focusing on agile delivery, modern architectural standards, and talent development. The move has enabled Capital One to achieve more frequent releases, reduce errors, and build a new development environment in minutes, leading to cost savings and enhanced innovation.

feed.title: Explainer: What is Parler and why has it been pulled offline?
feed.summary: Parler, a social media network founded in 2018 as a "free speech-driven" space, has been largely adopted by U.S. conservatives. It was taken offline after Amazon suspended its web hosting service, following similar bans by Apple and Google. These companies cited Parler's failure to prevent the spread of posts inciting violence, particularly after the U.S. Capitol unrest, leading Parler to sue Amazon.

feed.title: Oracle Debuts Major Release Of Its Flagship Database, First Since 2019
feed.summary: Oracle has launched its new 21c Database, the first major release of its flagship database since 2019, incorporating features from a planned 2020 version that was delayed due to the COVID-19 pandemic. The update includes over 200 enhancements such as automated machine learning, blockchain tables for immutability, and persistent memory capabilities. Additionally, Oracle is making its APEX low-code development tools available as a managed cloud service on Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI) to help developers build data-driven applications more quickly and affordably.

feed.title: Valvoline Launches Bag for Oil
feed.summary: Valvoline Inc. has introduced FlexFill, a new gear-oil packaging innovation designed to simplify the automotive DIY experience. This patented bag aims to make changing synthetic gear oil easier in tight spaces and reduce product waste compared to traditional hard plastic bottles. FlexFill is available in two synthetic gear oil grades and can be purchased nationwide and online.

feed.title: Amazon, Walmart, Target allowing some customers to keep refunded return items
feed.summary: Major retailers like Amazon, Walmart, and Target are implementing "returnless refunds," allowing some customers to keep items for which they receive a refund. This strategy aims to reduce shipping and handling costs associated with returns, especially for low-value merchandise or in situations where it's more convenient than processing a return. The move is also influenced by the COVID-19 pandemic, encouraging contactless transactions and discouraging non-essential travel to return goods.

feed.title: 5 reasons General Motors' shares are trading at all-time highs
feed.summary: General Motors' shares reached all-time highs due to investor confidence in its future business plans, particularly in electric vehicles (EVs). CEO Mary Barra outlined ambitious strategies at the CES technology conference, including a $27 billion investment in EV and autonomous vehicle technology, introducing the BrightDrop commercial EV division, and showcasing an electric air taxi concept. The company's Ultium battery system and continued development in autonomous vehicles also contribute to the positive market outlook.

feed.title: Amazon cries foul as trade office names ‘notorious markets’
feed.summary: The Office of the U.S. Trade Representative (USTR) has again included several Amazon.com Inc. foreign domains on its 2020 list of "notorious markets" for counterfeit goods, leading Amazon to label the move as politically motivated. Amazon claims it does more to combat counterfeiting than any other private entity and views its inclusion as a "personal vendetta" linked to battles between President Donald Trump and Amazon owner Jeff Bezos. The USTR report, however, cites complaints from U.S. companies regarding Amazon's inadequate vetting of sellers and policing of violations on its platforms in various countries.

feed.title: Poshmark surges 150% on its first day of trading after $277 million IPO
feed.summary: Poshmark's stock surged as much as 150% on its Thursday trading debut after its $277 million IPO on Wednesday. The online marketplace for second-hand goods sold 6.6 million shares at $42 each, following an initial marketing range of $35-$39. This successful debut continues a trend of strong investor demand for recent IPOs and SPACs, giving Poshmark a market capitalization over $7 billion.

feed.title: Disney+ Streaming App: Supported Devices, Bundles & Content
feed.summary: Disney+ launched on November 12, 2019, offering a vast library of content from Disney, Pixar, Marvel, Star Wars, and National Geographic, alongside original series like "The Mandalorian." The service provides a premium, commercial-free viewing experience with multiple streams and profiles, available on various devices. It also offers a bundle option with Hulu and ESPN+ for U.S. subscribers and plans for a global rollout.

feed.title: Why Petco CEO Ron Coughlin Says This IPO Is Different
feed.summary: Petco has gone public for the third time, with CEO Ron Coughlin asserting this IPO is different due to the company's strategic focus on integrating retail stores with services, investing in e-commerce, and paying down debt. While Petco faces stiff competition from major e-commerce players like Amazon and Chewy, Coughlin highlights its Vital Care program as a differentiator, combining veterinary care with other offerings. The IPO proceeds will fuel growth initiatives and reduce debt, aiming to strengthen Petco's position in the evolving pet care market.

feed.title: IBM buys cloud consultancy firm Taos Mountain
feed.summary: IBM has acquired Taos Mountain LLC, a multicloud consulting firm, to strengthen its hybrid cloud capabilities. This acquisition is part of IBM CEO Arvind Krishna's strategy to focus on hybrid cloud computing and help enterprises manage complex multicloud environments. Analysts view this move as IBM filling gaps in its services and enhancing its presence in a competitive market as more companies shift IT workloads to the cloud.

feed.title: Shopify founder injecting investment dollars into B.C.’s General Fusion
feed.summary: Shopify founder Tobias Lutke and his wife, Fiona McKean, are investing in Burnaby-based General Fusion Inc. through their venture capital firm, Thistledown Capital Inc. The investment will support the development of General Fusion’s demonstration plant, which aims to showcase its clean energy technology and is a stepping stone to commercialization. This is part of a broader trend of cleantech investments by Thistledown Capital, which has also included an investment in CarbonCure Technologies.

feed.title: Petco goes public again as spending on dogs and cats soars
feed.summary: Petco went public for the third time, capitalizing on the increased spending on pets by Americans treating them like family members. The company's stock soared on its IPO day, valuing it at over $6 billion, and it plans to use the $864 million raised to pay down debt. Petco has been rebranding to offer more services beyond just pet food, including vet services, grooming, and training, and has seen a revenue increase during the pandemic despite reporting a loss.

feed.title: Valero, USAA among San Antonio companies that donated to Republicans who voted to overturn presidential election results
feed.summary: Two major San Antonio employers, Valero and USAA, are re-evaluating their political spending after the Jan. 6 Capitol assault, having donated significant sums to Republicans who voted to overturn the 2020 presidential election results. Valero has halted all political contributions, while USAA is re-evaluating its criteria. Other local companies like NuStar Energy and iHeartMedia also made substantial donations to these Republicans.

feed.title: Conservative ETF Reshuffles, ‘QQQ’ Sibs Hit $1B
feed.summary: The American Conservative Values ETF (ACVF) has ceased new purchases of Apple, Amazon, and Google stock due to their perceived censorship of free speech, following their blocking of social media platform Parler. Meanwhile, Invesco's NASDAQ 100 ETF (QQQM) and Invesco NASDAQ Next Gen 100 ETF (QQQJ) have jointly surpassed $1 billion in assets under management in just three months, marking Invesco's most successful launch ever.

feed.title: Amazon workers in Alabama to vote on union by mail starting in February
feed.summary: Amazon workers at an Alabama fulfillment center will vote by mail in February and March on whether to join the Retail, Wholesale and Department Store Union, a decision made by the National Labor Relations Board. Amazon, which has largely avoided unions in the U.S., had argued for an in-person vote despite the ongoing pandemic. This marks Amazon's first union vote in the U.S. since 2014, and comes amidst increased scrutiny of working conditions during the pandemic.

feed.title: How self-storage became a $22 billion business
feed.summary: The self-storage industry, a $22 billion business, is experiencing shifting demand due to the coronavirus pandemic and historical drivers like the "four D's" (death, divorce, displacement, and disaster). Major players like Public Storage (PSA) and Extra Space Storage (EXR) are navigating these changes, with Public Storage reporting a revenue drop in Q3 2020 but seeing new demand from a robust housing market and urban migration. The industry also faces disruption from on-demand storage companies like Neighbor and Clutter.

feed.title: Monster Unveils 2021 Product Slate But No Hard Seltzer Yet
feed.summary: Monster Beverage Corporation did not announce a hard seltzer product during its 2021 Virtual Investors Meeting, despite rising speculation and previous reports of trademark filings. Instead, the company announced new products including slim cans of existing SKUs, line extensions, and redesigns, along with a co-CEO appointment. Monster also reported strong sales growth for the first nine months of 2020 but acknowledged being outpaced by some competitors in dollar sales growth.

feed.title: Walmart head of e-commerce Marc Lore leaving amid the retailer’s showdown with Amazon
feed.summary: Marc Lore, Walmart's head of e-commerce and the founder of Jet.com, is leaving the company on January 31, 2021, and will stay on as a strategic adviser through September. His departure comes as Walmart's competition with Amazon intensifies. Lore was instrumental in revamping Walmart's digital operations after its acquisition of Jet.com in 2016, though not all his initiatives were successful, leading to some tensions and eventual streamlining within the company.

feed.title: FedEx slaps new fee on big shippers, fueled by e-commerce
feed.summary: FedEx is implementing a new surcharge of 30 cents per residential package for large shippers, those averaging over 30,000 U.S. packages weekly, starting February 15. This fee is attributed to increased pandemic-related costs and sustained high demand for shipping capacity, reflecting the couriers' power to dictate prices due to the e-commerce boom. Analysts view this move positively for both FedEx and UPS, which have seen significant profit increases despite rising operational expenses.

feed.title: Netgear Nighthawk XR1000 Gaming Router Review: Congestion Control at Premium Price
feed.summary: The Netgear Nighthawk XR1000 is a high-end gaming router featuring DumaOS 3.0, which offers granular network traffic prioritization and Wi-Fi 6 support. Despite a bumpy setup, a single USB port, and a premium price that includes additional security subscription costs, its congestion control capabilities significantly optimize gaming performance in a busy network. The router is recommended for gamers prioritizing fine-tuned controls over flashy aesthetics.

feed.title: Instacart, Trader Joe’s and other businesses push incentives to get workers vaccinated
feed.summary: Several companies, including Instacart, Trader Joe's, and Dollar General, are offering financial incentives to their employees to encourage them to get vaccinated against COVID-19. This initiative comes as vaccination efforts roll out across the U.S. and some workers express reluctance. While companies can mandate vaccination, many are opting for incentives to ensure business continuity and protect their workforce.

feed.title: PepsiCo introduces bubbly drops for SodaStream
feed.summary: PepsiCo Inc. has launched bubly drops, a line of flavored sparkling water flavorings, for SodaStream machines. This collaboration aims to promote sustainability and reduce single-use plastic, offering six no-calorie, no-sweetener, and no-artificial-flavor options. The bubly drops are available at major retailers and online, expanding SodaStream's sustainable offerings.

feed.title: Does a Staples/Office Depot merger now make more sense?
feed.summary: Staples is attempting to acquire Office Depot for the third time, proposing a $2.1 billion deal. This renewed effort comes as the office supply retail landscape has drastically shifted due to increased online shopping and work-from-home trends, intensifying competition from Amazon. The article and expert commentary discuss whether this merger, previously blocked due to antitrust concerns, makes more sense now given the altered market dynamics and Office Depot's declining revenues.

feed.title: Lee and Wrangler looking to transform India business into omni-channel entities
feed.summary: American jeans brands Lee and Wrangler are transitioning their India businesses to an omni-channel model, aiming to double sales within two years by focusing on online channels. This shift involves moving from a fully owned subsidiary to a licensing deal with Bengaluru-based Ace Turtle, an e-commerce solutions provider, to integrate online and offline sales and rapidly expand their digital presence. Ace Turtle plans to increase local sourcing to 95% and optimize the supply chain for quicker online order fulfillment.

feed.title: Online TV Streaming Service Market: Strong Growth Continues
feed.summary: The online TV streaming service market is experiencing strong growth, driven by technological advancements, urbanization, increased internet/mobile subscriptions, and rising disposable incomes. Key players like Netflix have significantly expanded their subscriber base. The COVID-19 pandemic further accelerated demand, leading to a surge in subscriptions for major streaming platforms.

feed.title: The bull chasing the bandit: Webull takes on Robinhood
feed.summary: This article discusses how Webull, a Chinese-owned digital brokerage, is gaining traction in the US market as a competitor to Robinhood. Webull differentiates itself with a more professional platform, in-depth data, desktop support, and live customer service, appealing to seasoned investors while Robinhood faces regulatory challenges and criticism for its "gamification" of investing. Despite being Chinese-owned, Webull has largely flown under the regulatory radar due to its proactive government relations and low-key marketing strategy.

feed.title: 1M-SF Amazon fulfillment center in Brookshire trades hands
feed.summary: Exan Capital has acquired an 84-acre property at 31555 Highway 90 in Brookshire, which houses a 1M-SF Amazon fulfillment center. The undisclosed purchase was made from an unnamed publicly traded real estate investment trust. This acquisition is part of a series of related developments in the region, including other Amazon facilities and new distribution centers by companies like Lowe's and Goya Foods.

feed.title: Amazon likely tenant for 143,000-square-foot building coming to Greater Cincinnati
feed.summary: Amazon is expected to be the tenant for a new 143,000-square-foot distribution facility planned for West Chester Township. This development suggests Amazon's continued expansion in the Greater Cincinnati area. The article highlights Amazon's potential role as the nation's largest online retailer in this new distribution center.

feed.title: Why T-Shirts Promoting the Capitol Riot Are Still Available Online (Published 2021)
feed.summary: Following the Capitol riot, several e-commerce platforms like Amazon, Etsy, and Shopify struggled to remove merchandise promoting the attack and related conspiracy theories. While some companies eventually took down offensive items, many products, including "Battle for Capitol Hill Veteran" shirts, remained available, highlighting the platforms' challenges in monitoring third-party sellers. This situation put increased scrutiny on e-commerce companies' oversight of the goods sold through their sites.

feed.title: World's top 10 most valuable companies
feed.summary: Apple is the most valuable company globally, with a valuation of $2.12 trillion, followed by Microsoft and Amazon. The 2020 Hurun Global 500 report also highlighted that 51 Chinese companies made the list, with their total value increasing significantly. Electric carmakers Nio and Xpeng Motors, along with Beike Zhaofang, were among the top gainers in percentage growth.

feed.title: Amazon Prime member total reaches 142 million in U.S. with more shoppers opting in for a full year, data shows
feed.summary: Amazon's Prime membership in the U.S. reached 142 million as of December 31, 2020. Data from CIRP indicates a trend towards full-year memberships, with 52% of members holding an annual subscription, up from 49% three months prior. This suggests that while monthly sign-ups spike around the holidays for perks like free shipping, a larger proportion of members are committing to the annual plan.

feed.title: Aurora gets self-driving heavy truck deal as it wraps up buy of Uber unit
feed.summary: Aurora Innovation has secured a strategic partnership with Paccar, a major truck manufacturer, shortly after completing its acquisition of Uber's Advanced Technology Group. The Mountain View-based self-driving tech company now boasts over 1,600 employees across eight locations. This deal positions Aurora to further develop and test its self-driving heavy truck technology.

feed.title: Google Defends Against Allegations Of Collusion With Facebook On Ads
feed.summary: Adam Cohen, Google's director of economic policy, has refuted claims of collusion with Facebook regarding ad-sharing agreements, calling allegations by Texas attorney general Ken Paxton "misleading." The defense comes after a New York Times expose detailed an antitrust lawsuit suggesting Google provided special advantages to Facebook. Cohen clarified that Facebook competes in a crowded online advertising space and that their Open Bidding program is not manipulated in Facebook's favor, constituting less than 4% of Google's display ads.

feed.title: Amazon offers to help Biden speed delivery of COVID vaccines
authors: Spencer Soper and Matt Day and Bloomberg
feed.summary: Amazon has offered to assist the Biden administration in accelerating COVID-19 vaccine distribution, leveraging its operational and technological expertise. The company also requested that its frontline workers, numbering over 800,000 in the U.S., receive vaccines at the earliest appropriate time. This offer comes as Amazon faces scrutiny over its growing power and its handling of the pandemic, with some employees and officials criticizing its response to earlier outbreaks among staff.

feed.title: New Publishing Market Research 2021-2025 | COVID-19 Market Analysis and Recovery Report | Technavio
feed.summary: Technavio's latest market research report indicates a negative short-term growth in the publishing market due to COVID-19, with a projected decline of USD 18.69 billion between 2021-2025. Despite the growth of e-books, the traditional segment saw maximum growth in 2020, but the digital segment is expected to outpace it in the forecast period. APAC will drive 59% of the growth, fueled by technology adoption in education in countries like India and China, while the market remains fragmented with established vendors.

feed.title: Reliance on cloud, APIs create confusion and introduce risk into software development
feed.summary: A survey by Radware and Osterman Research reveals that businesses face significant challenges in securing software development due to increased reliance on cloud hosting and APIs. Less than half of companies have integrated security into their CI/CD pipelines, and many struggle with confusion regarding cloud provider responsibilities and API security. The report emphasizes the need for stronger partnerships between development and security teams, and greater investment in API security solutions to manage new attack vectors.

feed.title: Site appears to be Amazon.com’s 12th in Northeast Florida
feed.summary: Amazon.com is planning its 12th Northeast Florida facility, a delivery station identified as DJX7, at the former Harte Hanks marketing facility near The Avenues mall in South Jacksonville. The city is reviewing plans for the 132,000-square-foot warehouse space, which will process packages for final distribution to customers and include fleet parking for company vehicles. This new facility joins six other Amazon delivery stations either open or in review across Jacksonville and St. Johns County.

feed.title: Netgear Orbi 4G LTE (LBR20), hands-on: A versatile router with mesh Wi-Fi, mobile broadband and wired connectivity
feed.summary: The Netgear Orbi 4G LTE (LBR20) is a versatile router offering both wired Ethernet and 4G LTE mobile broadband connectivity, making it suitable for locations without traditional internet access or as a backup. It can function as a standalone Wi-Fi router or integrate into an existing Orbi mesh system, providing up to 2.2Gbps Wi-Fi and 1.2Gbps mobile broadband speeds. Priced at £370/$399.99, it supports failover to mobile broadband, ensuring continuous internet access.

feed.title: EXCLUSIVE: Amazon Looking to Locate 1 Million Square Foot Facility in Johnston
feed.summary: Amazon is reportedly in negotiations to acquire approximately 100 acres near Routes 6 and 295 in Johnston, Rhode Island, to build a 1 million square foot warehouse facility. This move aligns with Amazon's broader strategy to establish numerous smaller delivery hubs across the U.S. to enhance delivery speed and compete with Walmart. Local officials have declined to comment, and Amazon has not responded to inquiries regarding the potential development.

feed.title: Microsoft Is Leasing The Most Data Center Capacity In US
feed.summary: Microsoft led U.S. data center capacity leasing in 2020, with capacity tripling nationwide compared to 2019. The company leased 178 megawatts in Virginia alone and is expanding its global data center footprint to support Microsoft Azure. Other major leasers included Bytedance (TikTok), Facebook, Oracle, Google, and Twitter, driven by the shift to remote work during the COVID-19 pandemic.

feed.title: The evolution of the shopping mall: What consumers want
feed.summary: The article discusses the challenges faced by traditional shopping malls due to the rise of e-commerce and declining in-store traffic. It highlights consumer desires, identified through a survey, for new mall concepts focusing on food, health and wellness, and unique experiences to revitalize these properties. The authors emphasize the importance for developers, owners, and investors to adapt to these evolving consumer expectations to maximize revenue and ensure the malls' relevance as community hubs.

feed.title: Global Data Center Market Research 2021-2025 | Market Impact Analysis due to COVID-19 | Technavio
feed.summary: The global data center market is projected to grow by USD 519.34 billion between 2021 and 2025, with a CAGR exceeding 21%. This growth is primarily driven by the increasing adoption of multi-cloud strategies and necessary network upgrades to support 5G technology. The report analyzes the market's components and geographical landscape, offering insights into market sizing, forecast, and vendor analysis, while also considering the impact of COVID-19.

feed.title: Aisera’s Muddu Sudhakar looks beyond compute and storage for new opportunity
feed.summary: Muddu Sudhakar, a serial entrepreneur and CEO of Aisera, believes the next wave in tech will focus on service delivery models and AI rather than just compute and storage. He envisions enterprise workloads largely shifting to the cloud due to economic benefits and sees cloud providers dominating the SaaS market. Sudhakar's company, Aisera, is focused on robotic process automation and plans to leverage remote collaboration technology and AI in this evolving landscape.

feed.title: Constellation’s Holger Mueller on Salesforce, Oracle, AWS and the future of cloud software
feed.summary: Holger Mueller, vice president and principal analyst at Constellation Research Inc., discusses major trends in the cloud industry, including Salesforce's evolution into a platform company with the Slack acquisition. He also touches on Oracle's strong competitive position with its database focus and AWS's strategy in bridging cloud with on-premises workloads. Mueller foresees SaaS models continuing to drive cost reduction and highlights the increasing importance of AI and machine learning in the future of cloud software.

feed.title: How to Negotiate for a Higher Salary and Better Perks on a Job Offer
feed.summary: This article provides detailed advice on how to effectively negotiate a job offer, including salary and benefits, based on insights from Candor cofounder Niya Dragova. Key strategies include starting the negotiation early, avoiding disclosing salary expectations upfront, thoroughly understanding the full benefits package, and always negotiating via phone or video calls with a collaborative approach. The piece emphasizes that negotiation is always possible, even during economic slowdowns, and rarely leads to a rescinded offer.

feed.title: Sumo Citrus Season Is Back — Here's Where To Buy The Oranges Everyone Is Talking About
feed.summary: Sumo Citrus oranges, also known as "dekopon" or oversized mandarins, are back in season from January through April. They are popular for their sweet flavor, lack of seeds, and easy-to-peel rind, despite being pricier than regular oranges. The article advises consumers on where to purchase them, including Trader Joe's, Whole Foods, Sprouts, Kroger, and Publix, and reminds them to follow COVID-19 safety guidelines.

feed.title: Retail marketers invest in livestreams to establish shopping’s ‘next normal’
feed.summary: Retail marketers are increasingly using shoppable livestreams to engage consumers and drive e-commerce sales, especially as the pandemic keeps shoppers at home. Major brands like Ulta Beauty, Walmart, and Anne Klein are leveraging these interactive mobile experiences to demonstrate products and build direct customer relationships. The trend, already popular in Asia, is gaining traction in the U.S. with platforms like Amazon Live, Shoploop, and TikTok, showing significant growth potential.

feed.title: ProShares Launches Firm’s First Leveraged Thematic ETFs
feed.summary: ProShares has launched its first leveraged thematic investing ETFs: ProShares Ultra Nasdaq Cybersecurity (UCYB) and Ultra Nasdaq Cloud Computing (SKYU). These ETFs aim to deliver two times (2x) the daily performance of their respective underlying indexes,

feed.title: Eli Lilly's antibody drug reduces COVID-19 in nursing homes in latest study
feed.summary: Eli Lilly's antibody drug, bamlanivimab, significantly reduced the risk of symptomatic COVID-19 among residents and staff in long-term care facilities during a Phase 3 trial. The study showed up to an 80% lower risk of contracting the virus, with four placebo recipients dying from COVID-19. This positive outcome strengthens the case for expanding the drug's emergency use authorization to prevent the spread of COVID-19 in such vulnerable populations.

feed.title: Inauguration protests damage Seattle's Original Starbucks, Amazon Go store
feed.summary: Inauguration protests in Seattle resulted in property damage to several buildings, including the original Starbucks and an Amazon Go store. Police arrested three suspects for property damage, burglary, and felony assault, as the group vandalized buildings, used smoke canisters, and created road obstructions. The protests, which involved burning an American flag and carrying anti-Biden and anti-police signs, reflected similar incidents in Portland following the inauguration.

feed.title: Walmart to add Western Union services to stores
feed.summary: Walmart is expanding its financial service offerings by integrating Western Union money transfers, bill payment, and money order services into over 4,700 U.S. locations starting in spring 2021. This partnership builds on Walmart's broader strategy to enhance financial solutions for its customers, including a new fintech company with Ribbit Capital and existing collaborations with Affirm and Green Dot. The move also places Western Union alongside MoneyGram in Walmart stores, further solidifying the retailer's role in consumer financial transactions.

feed.title: C3 AI Ex Machina: No-Code AI that Anyone Can Use
feed.summary: C3 AI has launched C3 AI Ex Machina, a no-code AI platform designed to enable business and data analysts to develop, scale, and apply AI insights without writing code. This platform addresses the shortage of skilled data scientists and the limitations of existing no-code tools by offering comprehensive end-to-end capabilities for data ingestion, preparation, AI/ML model configuration, and the publication of results to drive business outcomes. It runs on a distributed, cloud-native web interface, ensuring scalability and cost management for enterprises.

feed.title: National Museum of African American Music receives donation from HCA Healthcare
feed.summary: The National Museum of African American Music (NMAAM) has received a donation from HCA Healthcare. This contribution highlights the importance of recognizing the significant impact African Americans have had on shaping American culture, particularly through music. NMAAM is unique in its dedication to preserving and celebrating over 50 genres and styles of music created or influenced by African Americans.

feed.title: Cut Complexity – Get Rapid, Automated Firewall Deployment in Azure
feed.summary: Palo Alto Networks has introduced Panorama Orchestrated VM-Series Firewall Deployments in Azure, simplifying firewall deployment and management. This new workflow eliminates the need for complex templates, enabling rapid deployment, scaling, and monitoring of VM-Series virtual firewalls in Azure. The solution aims to reduce user error, boost cost-effectiveness, and maintain consistent security through simplified operations within the Azure environment.

feed.title: Marketplaces: bancos y fintech crecen de la mano de sus tiendas on line
feed.summary: The article discusses how banks and fintech companies in Argentina are increasingly launching their own online marketplaces, driven by the pandemic and changing consumer habits. These platforms aim to foster commerce, build customer loyalty, and offer financial services. While not directly competing with giants like Mercado Libre, these initiatives like "Tienda Ciudad" and "Tienda Naranja" have seen significant growth, demonstrating a new trend in the financial sector.

feed.title: Corsair HS75 XB Wireless review
feed.summary: The Corsair HS75 XB Wireless gaming headset offers exceptional sound, comfort, and a strong wireless connection, making it a top choice for Xbox Series X/S and Xbox One users. However, its limited compatibility and additional adapter requirement for Windows 10 PCs, along with its high price point, might deter some potential buyers. Despite these drawbacks, its performance and design make it a contender for one of the best wireless Xbox headsets available.

feed.title: Young Industry, Old Roots | The 9 Veteran Startups of Quantum Tech
feed.summary: This article identifies and ranks nine veteran startups in the quantum technology space, challenging the common misconception about the oldest companies in the field. It highlights pioneers like D-Wave Systems, MagiQ Technologies, and even non-quantum-focused companies that pivoted into the sector, such as Oxford Instruments and High Precision Devices. The piece also acknowledges later entrants that established themselves despite early skepticism towards quantum technology.

feed.title: Fox Corporation appoints Varun Narang as Chief Product Officer
feed.summary: Fox Corporation has appointed Varun Narang as its new Chief Product Officer. In this role, Narang will oversee the company's FOX-branded streaming products and platforms, working to drive digital priorities and accelerate the growth of current and future digital streaming offerings. He previously served as Chief Product Officer of Disney+Hotstar and held senior positions at Hulu, Amazon, and Xerox.

feed.title: Long Island financial tech startup gets $1M from early Etsy funders
feed.summary: Bookkeeper360, a Long Island-based financial technology startup, has secured $1 million in funding from brothers Judson and Spencer Ain, who were early angel investors in Etsy Inc. The company offers a subscription-based financial app and services to small businesses, focusing on metrics like working capital and cash-burn analysis. This new investment aims to fuel Bookkeeper360's growth as it expands its integrations and services for e-commerce merchants and other small businesses.

feed.title: Press release: Groundbreaking Sony Alpha 1 Camera Marks a New Era in Professional Imaging
feed.summary: Sony Electronics has announced the Alpha 1, a new full-frame mirrorless camera combining high-resolution with high-speed performance, featuring a 50.1-megapixel stacked Exmor RS image sensor, 30fps continuous shooting, and 8K 30p video recording. The Alpha 1 also boasts an advanced electronic viewfinder with a 240 fps refresh rate, improved autofocus with Real-time Eye AF for birds, and enhanced connectivity for professional workflows. This camera is set to be available in March 2021 for approximately $6,500 USD.

feed.title: The SNPE ETF Has Outpaced the S&P 500 Since Its Inception
feed.summary: The Xtrackers S&P 500 ESG ETF (SNPE) has outperformed the S&P 500 since its inception, boasting a 35% gain compared to the S&P 500's 30%. The fund tracks the S&P 500 ESG Index, focusing on companies with strong environmental, social, and governance performance. Analysts are split on whether the ESG trend will continue its momentum, particularly with a new administration taking office.

feed.title: Walgreens names Starbucks executive Roz Brewer as CEO
feed.summary: Walgreens Boots Alliance Inc. has appointed Starbucks chief operating officer Rosalind Brewer as its next CEO, effective March 15. Brewer will be the only Black female leading a Fortune 500 company and previously led Starbucks' digital sales shift, technology overhaul, and pandemic response. She will replace Stefano Pessina, who will become executive chairman of the board.

feed.title: Qualcomm to supply General Motors with 'cockpit' chips for next generation vehicles
feed.summary: Qualcomm Inc announced a deal to supply General Motors Co with a range of chips for its next generation of vehicles. The agreement includes Qualcomm's "cockpit" chips for in-dashboard systems and automated driver assistance system chips. Qualcomm's Snapdragon Ride driver-assistance computer is also expected to be in a production vehicle in 2022.

feed.title: T. Rowe Price Blue Chip Growth's Manager to Retire
feed.summary: Larry Puglia, the long-standing manager of T. Rowe Price Blue Chip Growth (TRBCX) since its inception in 1993, is set to retire at the end of 2021. Paul Greene, who became associate portfolio manager in January 2020, will officially take over on October 1, 2021, ensuring a smooth transition. The strategy maintains Above Average Process and People ratings and a Silver Morningstar Analyst Rating, reflecting confidence in its continued success despite the managerial change.

feed.title: How Elon Musk Became the New Oprah--and Why That Matters to You
feed.summary: Elon Musk has developed an influence similar to Oprah Winfrey's "Oprah Effect," where a single endorsement can cause significant financial impact. An example of this is a tweet by Musk about Etsy, which led to a $1 billion increase in the company's market capitalization. This phenomenon, dubbed the "Musk Effect," demonstrates the power of his social media presence to drive market trends.

feed.title: Precious Metals Outlook 2021: Renewable Energy Will Be a Key Driver
feed.summary: In 2021, precious metals, especially silver, are expected to continue their strong performance due to the global transition towards renewable energy. Gold saw significant gains in 2020, but faces competition from cryptocurrencies like Bitcoin. The article also anticipates increased corporate activity in the gold mining sector and a growing role for platinum in hydrogen fuel cells, influenced by evolving automotive and energy policies.

feed.title: AWS MySQL: MySQL as a Service vs. Self Managed in the Cloud
feed.summary: This article explores options for running MySQL databases on AWS, comparing "MySQL as a Service" offerings like AWS RDS and Amazon Aurora with a self-managed approach using Amazon EC2 instances. It details the process of creating databases in RDS and Aurora, discusses the pros and cons of managed versus self-managed deployments, and introduces NetApp Cloud Volumes ONTAP as a solution to combine the benefits of both approaches for enterprise-grade MySQL in AWS.

feed.title: Buying Signavio, SAP gets into business transformation-as-a-service
feed.summary: SAP has launched a new offering called "RISE with SAP," which aims to provide "Business transformation-as-a-Service" to help enterprises achieve digital transformation by moving to a cloud and subscription-based model. As part of this initiative, SAP is acquiring Signavio GmbH, a company specializing in business process intelligence, to enhance its capabilities in business process redesign. The RISE with SAP service bundles contracts, SLAs, operations, and support into a single subscription, with customers already operating under this model.

feed.title: Neo4j unveils cloud graph database for the enterprise
feed.summary: Neo4j has launched Aura Enterprise, its first fully-managed cloud graph database specifically for large organizations, following the earlier release of Aura Professional for SMBs. This new offering allows enterprises to build graph applications at scale, manage large datasets with billions of nodes, and benefit from enterprise-grade security and consumption-based pricing. The move aims to make graph database technology more accessible by lowering adoption barriers and reducing the burden of infrastructure management for data scientists.

feed.title: Wipro finds its first Brazil head in former AWS exec Douglas Silva
feed.summary: Wipro has appointed Douglas Silva as its new country head for Brazil. Silva, a former AWS executive, brings 24 years of IT industry experience, having led AWS's enterprise business growth in Brazil and held roles at Hewlett Packard, TCS, Capgemini, and SAP. This appointment reflects Wipro's commitment to strengthening its presence and growth in the dynamic Latin American market, following strategic investments and acquisitions in the region.

feed.title: 2022 Acura MDX Review: Tech Pains Curb Family, Luxury Gains
feed.summary: The 2022 Acura MDX receives a redesign, enhancing its family-friendly refinement and comfort with a solid powertrain and improved road manners. While the interior offers upgraded materials and versatile seating options, including a removable second-row middle seat, its new multimedia system with console-area touchpads proves frustrating to use. Despite technology advancements like Amazon Alexa integration, the usability issues with the infotainment system slightly detract from an otherwise appealing luxury SUV package.

feed.title: Pro-Ocean: Rocke Group’s New Cryptojacking Malware
feed.summary: Unit 42 researchers have identified "Pro-Ocean," a revised cryptojacking malware used by the Rocke Group, which targets cloud applications to mine Monero. This updated malware features improved rootkit and worm capabilities, uses sophisticated hiding techniques to evade detection, and focuses on cloud providers in China. Pro-Ocean consists of four modules—hiding, mining, infecting, and watchdog—and exploits known vulnerabilities in Apache ActiveMQ, Oracle WebLogic, and Redis to achieve persistence and maximize CPU usage for mining.

feed.title: Reckitt invests in Bombay Shaving Co
feed.summary: Reckitt Benckiser has led a Rs 45-crore strategic investment in Bombay Shaving Company, demonstrating large consumer goods companies' interest in youth-oriented digital brands. This investment will help Bombay Shaving Co expand into offline retail and leverage Reckitt's expertise in market understanding and R&D. The grooming startup aims to reach 50,000 stores in 30 cities by the end of 2021 and plans to launch new personal care brands to broaden its market beyond male grooming.

feed.title: Walmart plans big push to challenge Amazon on advertising
feed.summary: Walmart is significantly expanding its advertising business, aiming to become a top 10 U.S. advertising platform within five years and challenge Amazon. The retailer plans to sell ads in its U.S. stores and share shopper data with brands through a new platform developed with Trade Desk Inc., allowing advertisers to target customers and measure sales effectiveness in real-time. This strategy leverages Walmart's physical stores and vast data to compete in the growing retail media space.

feed.title: Forget Procter & Gamble: Here Are 3 Better Dividend Stocks
feed.summary: This article suggests three dividend stocks—Stag Industrial, JPMorgan Chase, and American Electric Power—as better options than Procter & Gamble for income-focused investors. It highlights Stag Industrial's REIT structure and solid tenant base, JPMorgan Chase's financial resilience and growth in corporate/investment banking, and American Electric Power's strong dividend growth and proactive shift towards renewable energy sources. Each company's dividend yield, growth rate, and competitive advantages are detailed.

feed.title: Here are the biggest short squeezes in the stock market, including GameStop and AMC
feed.summary: The article discusses the biggest short squeezes observed in the stock market, focusing on GameStop and AMC Entertainment. It notes that dozens of heavily shorted stocks have seen significant increases in 2021, driven by individual investors on social media. GameStop was up 1,745% and AMC Entertainment 839% through January 27, 2021.

feed.title: Television makers see brighter prospects
feed.summary: China's television manufacturing industry expects an 8.4% revenue increase to 131.1 billion yuan ($20.3 billion) in 2021, driven by new-generation flat-panel display technologies and consumption upgrades. Despite a 9.1% unit sales drop in 2020 due to COVID-19, online sales soared, and larger, intelligent TVs along with advanced display technologies like 8K, QLED, OLED, and Micro LED are fueling market growth and competition among manufacturers.

feed.title: HRC Index: 51 Retailers Named Best Places to Work for LGBTQ Equality
feed.summary: The Human Rights Campaign (HRC) Foundation's 2021 Corporate Equality Index (CEI) recognized 51 retail companies as "Best Places to Work for LGBTQ Equality" for achieving a 100% ranking. The retail industry continues to be a leader in LGBTQ workplace equality, with significant progress noted in transgender-inclusive initiatives. The CEI criteria assess non-discrimination policies, equitable benefits, inclusive culture, and corporate social responsibility.

feed.title: Walmart plans big push to challenge Amazon on advertising
feed.summary: Walmart announced plans to significantly expand its advertising business, aiming to become a top 10 U.S. advertising platform within five years and challenge Amazon's dominance. The retailer will add ad space in its U.S. stores and leverage its shopper data to make ads more effective through a partnership with Trade Desk Inc. This strategy focuses on in-house advertising and utilizing its physical presence and vast data to offer measurable ad solutions to brands.

feed.title: Amazon Keeps Buying Pricey Jets to Meet Customer Demand
feed.summary: Amazon is significantly expanding its Amazon Air cargo fleet by purchasing 11 Boeing 767-300 jets to meet surging customer demand for one- and two-day shipping, especially after the pandemic exacerbated the strain on traditional carriers like UPS and FedEx. This expansion, while costly, is deemed a necessity to maintain delivery pledges and overcome logistical bottlenecks. The company is also opening a $1.5 billion cargo terminal in Cincinnati/Northern Kentucky airport to support its growing air operations.

feed.title: Costco logistics center would be Lewis County's largest building
feed.summary: Costco is considering building a 1.5 million-square-foot logistics center in Lewis County, which would be the largest building in the county. This project, if it moves forward, would be the third major distribution center for Lewis County, a strategic location between Seattle and Portland. The Port of Chehalis is currently studying the feasibility of this massive development.

feed.title: BI vendor Phocas raises funds to build predictive analytics
feed.summary: Phocas Software, an Australian-based BI vendor, secured $34 million in venture capital funding to expand its presence, further develop its analytics products, and accelerate growth in the U.S. and U.K. The funding will support the company's move into predictive analytics and new industry verticals. Phocas, which was previously self-funded for 20 years, aims to reach $100 million in revenue.

feed.title: Amazon Can Make Just About Anything—Except a Good Video Game
feed.summary: Amazon's gaming division, Amazon Game Studios, has struggled to produce a successful video game despite significant investment and hiring top talent. Mike Frazzini, who leads the division, has overseen multiple failures and cancellations, highlighting a rare area where Amazon's business philosophy has not translated into success. This stands in contrast to Amazon's achievements in other diverse sectors like movies, TV, and electronics.

feed.title: Grape-Nuts prices skyrocket online amid coronavirus-caused shortage
feed.summary: Grape-Nut cereal prices are surging online due to a product shortage caused by supply constraints and increased demand during the coronavirus pandemic. A four-pack of 29-ounce boxes was seen selling for $54.99 on Amazon and a 64-ounce box for $110.90 on Walmart.com, significantly higher than the suggested retail price of $4.99 per 29-ounce box. Post Consumer Brands expects Grape-Nuts to return to stores in March 2021.

feed.title: Amazon, Alphabet and Salesforce are all investing in a $28 billion company that crunches big data
feed.summary: Databricks, a company specializing in big data processing and AI model deployment, has raised $1 billion in new funding, bringing its valuation to $28 billion. Prominent investors include Amazon Web Services, Alphabet's CapitalG, Salesforce Ventures, and existing investor Microsoft. This investment highlights the recognition from top cloud providers of Databricks' potential, similar to Snowflake, in helping companies manage and analyze large datasets.

feed.title: Powered by Android: Ford Motor Company's Future Cars Will Have Google On Board
feed.summary: Ford Motor Company has announced a six-year partnership with Google, integrating Android as the operating system for its future in-vehicle connectivity starting in 2023. This collaboration will give Ford and Lincoln owners access to Google services like Maps, Play, and Assistant, and allow third-party apps. The deal aims to streamline Ford's operations and support its $11 billion restructuring plan by leveraging Google's expertise in in-car entertainment and navigation systems.

feed.title: Walmart grocery delivery reaches 3,000 stores
