feed.summary: Walmart has expanded its same-day grocery delivery service to 3,000 stores, nearly doubling its coverage in the past year to meet increased demand and support its Walmart+ membership program. The retailer is waiving delivery fees for new customers on three orders of at least $50 to encourage adoption. This expansion positions Walmart to compete more effectively with Amazon in the online grocery market.

feed.title: Nestle, Nvidia, and the Changing Global Equity Markets
feed.summary: The article analyzes the shifting dynamics of global equity markets over the past decade, characterized by the dominance of U.S. technology stocks like Nvidia, in contrast to traditional companies like Nestle. It highlights the increased concentration of U.S. market indexes in technology and consumer cyclical sectors, and discusses current valuation opportunities in non-U.S. markets and value sectors like energy.

feed.title: Apple (AAPL), Microsoft (MSFT), Disney (DIS) Are Top Admired Companies
feed.summary: Apple, Microsoft, and Disney have been recognized as the top admired companies in Fortune Magazine's 2021 list, securing the first, third, and fourth spots respectively. This ranking, compiled in partnership with Korn Ferry, surveyed executives, directors, and analysts on various criteria. While direct financial impact is minimal, such accolades are valuable as recruiting tools to attract top talent.

feed.title: Amazon’s Jeff Bezos to step down as CEO later this year
feed.summary: Amazon announced that its founder and CEO, Jeff Bezos, will step down from his role later this year and transition to executive chairman. Andy Jassy, who currently leads Amazon’s cloud business, will replace him as CEO. Bezos stated that the company is at its most inventive, making it an optimal time for this transition, and he plans to focus on other ventures including The Washington Post.

feed.title: Amazon founder Jeff Bezos to step down as CEO
feed.summary: Jeff Bezos, founder of Amazon, will step down as CEO in the third quarter of 2021 and transition to executive chairman, with Andy Jassy, head of the cloud computing unit, taking over as CEO. This transition comes as Amazon reports record sales and profits, particularly from its cloud services, indicating a strong position for leadership change. Bezos stated that Amazon is "at its most inventive ever," making it an optimal time for this shift.

feed.title: AWS chief Andy Jassy to succeed Amazon founder Jeff Bezos as CEO
feed.summary: Andy Jassy, the long-time head of Amazon Web Services (AWS), has been named the successor to Amazon founder and CEO Jeff Bezos. Bezos will transition to executive chair in the third quarter of the year, intending to focus on new products and early initiatives. Jassy, who built AWS into a dominant force in cloud computing, is seen as a natural choice, having been groomed for the role and demonstrating strong leadership and financial success within Amazon.

feed.title: Pandemic cloud migration push to have lasting impact
feed.summary: The COVID-19 pandemic significantly accelerated cloud migrations in 2020, leading to permanent changes in enterprise cloud strategies and spending habits. Gartner and IDC reports indicate a substantial increase in public cloud spending and a shift towards cloud-first approaches, driven by the need for cost optimization and commercial elasticity. The adoption of multi-cloud strategies and new billing models from vendors like IBM further reflect the lasting impact of the pandemic on cloud computing.

feed.title: Amazon's PenPlace plans call for three more HQ2 office buildings, soaring 'Helix' tower
feed.summary: Amazon has unveiled its plans for the next phase of its HQ2 in Arlington, Virginia, dubbed 'PenPlace'. The development will feature three new office buildings, retail spaces, public areas, a dog park, and a distinctive 370,000-square-foot tower called "The Helix." The design encompasses 2.8 million square feet of office space and over two acres of green space.

feed.title: Climb On Board the ESG Megatrend with 'EFIV'
feed.summary: The article highlights ESG investing as a "megatrend" and introduces the SPDR S&P 500 ESG ETF (EFIV) as a way for investors to access this market. It notes that President Joe Biden's administration is expected to provide tailwinds for ESG through its focus on clean energy and potential SEC reforms. EFIV invests in S&P 500 firms meeting sustainability criteria, maintaining similar industry group weights to the S&P 500, and has shown a 17% gain over the past year with a 0.10% expense ratio.

feed.title: AMAZON.COM ANNOUNCES FINANCIAL RESULTS AND CEO TRANSITION
feed.summary: Amazon.com, Inc. announced strong financial results for its fourth quarter and full year ended December 31, 2020, with significant increases in net sales and net income. Jeff Bezos will transition to the role of Executive Chair in Q3 2021, and Andy Jassy will become the new Chief Executive Officer of Amazon. The company also highlighted its investments in employee safety, community support, environmental initiatives, and empowering small and medium-sized businesses.

feed.title: JD’s Commitment to 100% Authentic Products
feed.summary: JD.com emphasizes its foundational commitment to authentic products, a principle established by founder Richard Liu in 1998. The company employs a three-pronged approach to combat counterfeits, involving end-to-end control through its self-developed logistics network, rigorous quality assurance including a "zero tolerance" policy for fakes, and advanced "smart brand protection" technology like "Heaven’s Mirror" and blockchain traceability. This comprehensive strategy aims to build and maintain consumer trust in its products.

feed.title: Payoneer Reaches $3.3 Billion Deal to Go Public With Betsy Cohen SPAC
feed.summary: Payoneer Inc., an online payments firm, has agreed to go public through a merger with FTAC Olympus Acquisition Corp., a blank-check company led by Betsy Cohen. The deal values Payoneer at $3.3 billion and includes a $300 million private investment in public equity (PIPE). Payoneer, which processed over $44 billion in payments last year and is used by companies like Airbnb and Amazon, is profitable and projects $432 million in revenue for 2021.

feed.title: Amazon’s delivery speed is facing increasing competition from rivals with a fleet of stores
feed.summary: Amazon's delivery dominance is being challenged by traditional retailers like Walmart and Target. These competitors are leveraging their physical store locations to act as fulfillment centers, enabling them to match Amazon's rapid delivery speeds. This development suggests a shift in competitive advantage in the e-commerce delivery landscape.

feed.title: Here’s how the FAANG companies stack up this earnings season
feed.summary: This article examines the performance of FAANG companies during the latest earnings season, highlighting their continued impressive growth in revenue and display of pricing power. Despite their large size and the challenges posed by the pandemic, these companies maintained successful growth strategies in the fourth quarter and throughout the previous year. The piece underscores the dominance of FAANG stocks in earnings reports.

feed.title: Here’s how the FAANG companies stack up this earnings season
feed.summary: The article discusses the strong performance of FAANG companies during the earnings season, specifically highlighting their impressive revenue growth and pricing power. This trend continued into the fourth quarter of the previous year and throughout the entire year, even amidst the pandemic. The author notes that these companies consistently deliver significant increases in revenue despite their large size.

feed.title: Amazon CEO Jeff Bezos may step down without stepping away
feed.summary: Jeff Bezos is stepping down as Amazon CEO this summer, transitioning to executive chairman while long-time Amazon executive Andy Jassy takes over the CEO role. Bezos intends to focus on new innovations and ventures like Blue Origin and The Washington Post, while Jassy will face increasing scrutiny over Amazon's market power and employee treatment. Despite the change in leadership, investors remained focused on Amazon's blockbuster earnings, and Bezos is expected to remain involved in key strategic decisions.

feed.title: Billion-dollar baby: Amazon daily sales surpass new milestone
feed.summary: Amazon.com Inc. achieved over $1 billion a day in revenue in 2020, reaching $386 billion for the full year, driven by the holiday shopping season and increased online shopping during the pandemic. The e-commerce giant's revenue surged by nearly 44% in the fourth quarter to over $100 billion, reflecting a significant shift towards online retail that analysts expect to continue. Despite CEO Jeff Bezos stepping down, strong analyst ratings and projected market dominance indicate continued growth for the company.

feed.title: NXP Launches Flexible IoT Cloud Platform to Securely Manage and Connect Edge Devices
Beer on Tap — Uber’s Acquisition of Drizly Sets up Big Gains (for Some) in Alcohol E-commerce
feed.summary: Uber Technologies, Inc. announced its acquisition of Drizly, the largest online alcohol marketplace in the U.S., for $1.1 billion. This acquisition is set to significantly expand Drizly's reach by integrating with Uber Eats, capitalizing on the booming alcohol e-commerce market which saw sales up 350% for Drizly in 2020. The deal highlights the increasing importance of online alcohol sales and raises questions about how it will impact different alcohol producers and retailers, especially concerning product visibility and delivery logistics.

feed.title: Charlie Scharf’s no-nonsense style could create a more stable Wells Fargo
feed.summary: This article discusses Wells Fargo CEO Charlie Scharf's no-nonsense management style, honed during his time with Jamie Dimon, as he works to stabilize the scandal-ridden bank. It also touches on NASDAQ CEO Adena Friedman's defense of new board diversity requirements and news of Jeff Bezos stepping down as Amazon CEO, with Andy Jassy taking over. Other topics include the effectiveness of Russia's Sputnik V vaccine, AstraZeneca's vaccine data, and the GameStop stock plunge.

feed.title: World’s Fastest-Growing Cloud Vendors: #1 Google, #2 Oracle, #3 Microsoft & ServiceNow, #5 Amazon
feed.summary: The article identifies the five fastest-growing major cloud vendors based on their Q4 growth rates and revenue. Google Cloud led with 46.5% growth, followed by Oracle, Microsoft, ServiceNow, and Amazon Web Services, collectively generating about $37 billion in Q4 cloud revenue. The author emphasizes the significance of these growth rates in the context of the unprecedented expansion of the cloud market and its profound global impact on various industries and daily life.

feed.title: Allsup’s increases accessibility and convenience
feed.summary: Allsup's in Olney is enhancing customer convenience by expanding its services. Customers can now use Western Union for money transfers, pick up Amazon packages from new Amazon Hub Lockers, and purchase the local Olney Enterprise newspaper inside the store, in addition to existing services like gas and hot meals. These new offerings aim to make Allsup's a more comprehensive one-stop-shop for the community.

feed.title: ‘Accelerated’: How Amazon plans to deploy electric vans
feed.summary: Amazon plans to deploy 100,000 electric delivery vans by 2030, with 10,000 on the road by 2022, starting in 15 major U.S. markets. This initiative is a key part of Amazon's strategy to significantly reduce its carbon footprint, moving towards majority zero-emission last-mile deliveries by 2030. The company is accelerating development with Rivian, building thousands of charging stations, and exploring diverse solutions for "middle mile" logistics, including hydrogen and natural gas.

feed.title: Meet Andy Jassy, the next CEO of Amazon
feed.summary: Andy Jassy is set to take over as Amazon's CEO from Jeff Bezos, a transition announced after 24 years of Jassy's tenure at the company. Jassy, who successfully grew Amazon Web Services into a $45 billion business, worked closely with Bezos for years, rising through the ranks to lead a crucial part of Amazon's enterprise. This article highlights his career trajectory, compensation, and the long-planned succession strategy behind his appointment.

feed.title: Why Amazon’s $62 Million FTC Labor Settlement Is a Bigger Deal Than the Bezos News
feed.summary: Amazon settled with the FTC for $62 million over allegations of withholding tips from its Flex delivery drivers, a sum that, while small compared to the company's profits, highlights growing labor and equity concerns. This settlement and ongoing unionization efforts at an Alabama warehouse are seen as significant challenges for Amazon and its new CEO, Andy Jassy, potentially leading to more serious consequences for future infractions. Experts suggest unionization could have prevented such issues, emphasizing the importance of collective bargaining for workers' rights and safety, especially amidst the COVID-19 pandemic.

feed.title: Harley-Davidson rides off from Amazon
feed.summary: Harley-Davidson announced its departure from Amazon Marketplace, citing concerns that the platform was undercutting sales at its own dealerships and not integrating them properly. This move comes as Harley-Davidson aims to strengthen its brand, pricing power, and digital e-commerce business alongside its dealers, following a period of declining sales. The motorcycle maker joins a growing list of iconic brands like Nike and Ikea that have left Amazon, often due to issues with counterfeits and unauthorized sellers, as well as Amazon's private-label expansion.

feed.title: Chris Lyons: Where Tech Meets Culture
feed.summary: Chris Lyons, head of Andreessen Horowitz's Cultural Leadership Fund, discusses how the fund connects Black cultural leaders with tech companies and elevates African-American creators in the tech industry. He explains the rationale behind the fund, its unique all-African-American LP structure, and its mission to foster entrepreneurship and generational wealth within the Black community by increasing access and exposure to technology and venture capital. Lyons also shares his personal journey from audio engineer to Silicon Valley investor, emphasizing the importance of networking and service.

feed.title: Microsoft halts donations through 2022 to lawmakers who opposed Biden certification
feed.summary: Microsoft announced it will suspend all political action committee (PAC) donations through 2022 to U.S. lawmakers and state officials who voted against certifying Joe Biden's election or supported overturning the results. This decision aligns with similar actions by other major U.S. companies like Walmart, Amazon, and Google, reflecting a broader business community response to the events surrounding the 2020 election. Microsoft President Brad Smith emphasized the role of PACs in building political relationships and the company's commitment to strengthening democracy.

feed.title: Netgear AX1800 Mesh Extender (EAX20) review
feed.summary: The Netgear AX1800 Mesh Extender (EAX20) is reviewed as a fast Wi-Fi 6 extender, offering extended range and security features, though it is noted for its large size, lack of a USB port, and high price. It offers strong performance, especially with Wi-Fi 6 compatible routers, and extensive customization through its app or browser interface. Despite some drawbacks, it is recommended for its top-tier performance in extending Wi-Fi networks.

feed.title: Netgear Orbi Voice Review: Mesh Favorite Adds Alexa
feed.summary: The Netgear Orbi Voice mesh router system integrates an Alexa voice assistant and Harman Kardon-tuned speakers into one of its satellites, offering excellent Wi-Fi performance alongside smart home capabilities. While its audio quality is described as muddy compared to dedicated speakers like the Sonos One, the system excels in network range and throughput. The $430 RBK50V kit combines a powerful router with a smart speaker extension, aiming to provide both robust internet and smart functionality despite its large size and average sound.

feed.title: Tessitura Network Taps Rackspace Technology to Accelerate
feed.summary: Tessitura Network, a non-profit serving arts and cultural organizations, partnered with Rackspace Technology to migrate its software hosting from a legacy collocated center to a managed AWS environment. This transition allows Tessitura to enhance scalability, monitoring, and security, freeing its engineers to focus on developing new features for its users. The collaboration enables Tessitura to maintain a competitive edge and better serve the arts community.

feed.title: Amazon orders more than 1,000 natural gas engines for U.S. truck fleet
feed.summary: Amazon has ordered over 1,000 truck engines powered by compressed natural gas for its U.S. fleet, aiming to reduce pollution from its delivery operations. These engines, supplied by a Cummins Inc. and Westport Fuel Systems Inc. joint venture, will be used in heavy-duty trucks transporting goods between warehouses and distribution centers. The move reflects Amazon's efforts to test sustainable freight transportation solutions, including electric and CNG vehicles, to address the increasing emissions from delivery vehicles.

feed.title: As Digital Payments Break $6 Trillion, Snag 'FINX'
feed.summary: The digital payments industry is projected to reach over $6 trillion by the end of 2021, driven by increased adoption during the pandemic and investments from tech giants. This growth presents an opportunity for fintech-focused exchange-traded funds like the Global X FinTech ETF (FINX), which offers exposure to companies at the forefront of financial technology innovation. FINX has significantly outperformed broader financial sector indexes, attributed to strong holdings like Square Inc, Afterpay Ltd, and Adyen NV.

feed.title: As Digital Payments Break $6 Trillion, Snag 'FINX'
feed.summary: The digital payments industry is projected to reach over $6 trillion by the end of 2021, driven by innovations like mobile wallets and the increasing adoption by big tech companies. This growth benefits fintech-focused funds such as the Global X FinTech ETF (FINX), which offers exposure to companies applying technological innovations to financial services. FINX has shown strong performance, significantly outperforming broader financial indexes, largely due to its top holdings like Square Inc, Afterpay Ltd, and Adyen NV.

feed.title: Amazon orders hundreds of trucks that run on natural gas
feed.summary: Amazon has placed orders for hundreds of trucks that operate on natural gas. This move is part of the company's efforts to expand its use of alternative fuels in its logistics operations. The orders indicate a push towards more sustainable transportation solutions within Amazon's vast shipping network.

feed.title: Sam’s Club teams up with Synchrony, Mastercard for rewards program
feed.summary: Sam's Club has partnered with Synchrony and Mastercard to launch an enhanced Sam's Club Mastercard rewards program, offering members more digital features and incentives. The program, announced on January 27, includes 5% cash back on eligible purchases for Sam's Club Plus members and features contactless payments, online rewards access, and mobile app integration. This initiative aims to help customers save money and earn rewards, especially in light of the past year's financial challenges.

feed.title: Tom Gayner Top 10 Holdings (Q4 2020)
feed.summary: This article details the top 10 holdings of superinvestor Tom Gayner's portfolio as of Q4 2020, based on his 13F-HR filing. The total market value of his portfolio was over $6.7 billion, with his top 10 holdings accounting for 42.79% of the total. The article also lists his top buys and sells during that period, including significant holdings in Berkshire Hathaway, Brookfield Asset Management, and The Walt Disney Co.

feed.title: Tyler Technologies' New Corporate Operations Team to Support a Multi-Year Cloud Strategy
feed.summary: Tyler Technologies has launched a new Corporate Operations team to spearhead its multi-year cloud strategy, aiming to help state, local, and federal government agencies transition to public cloud infrastructure. Led by newly appointed COO Jeff Puckett and VP Russell Gainford, the team will focus on cloud strategy, operations, and development to enhance security, communication, and software delivery for government clients. This initiative extends Tyler's two decades of SaaS offerings and strategic collaboration with Amazon Web Services, driven by accelerated cloud adoption during the COVID-19 pandemic.

feed.title: My Journey That Began at IIT (BHU) | Jay Chaudhry
feed.summary: Jay Chaudhry, CEO and Founder of Zscaler, reflects on his journey starting from IIT (BHU) in Varanasi. He shares key life lessons learned, including taking risks and pursuing passion, which led him through diverse roles at major companies and ultimately to founding multiple successful startups, culminating in Zscaler. The article highlights his path from an engineering student to a cybersecurity pioneer, emphasizing the foundational impact of his education and early experiences.

feed.title: Apollo-backed low-cost airline Sun Country files for U.S. IPO
feed.summary: Sun Country Airlines, a low-cost carrier supported by Apollo Global Management, has filed for an initial public offering (IPO) on the Nasdaq under the symbol "SNCY." The airline is betting on a recovery in air travel as COVID-19 vaccines become more widespread. Sun Country reported operating revenue of $293.7 million for the first nine months of 2020 and expects the airline industry to rebound in late 2021 and normalize in 2022.

feed.title: Power Corp. looks to vacate penthouse suite in Toronto’s Brookfield Place
feed.summary: Power Corp. of Canada is privately marketing its 17,000-square-foot penthouse office space at Brookfield Place in Toronto for sublease, a move mirroring a broader trend of financial services companies shedding prime real estate during the pandemic. The company, headquartered in Montreal, leases the top two floors of the Brookfield Place complex, with its lease set to expire in August 2024. This decision contributes to an increasing downtown Toronto office vacancy rate, which has risen to 7.2 percent from 2 percent pre-pandemic, as businesses re-evaluate their office needs amid remote work trends.

feed.title: Tesla Bets Big on Bitcoin, Starves its 401(k) Plan
feed.summary: Tesla announced a $1.5 billion investment in Bitcoin and plans to accept the cryptocurrency as payment, pushing Bitcoin to record highs. Despite this progressive financial move and significant stock growth, Tesla's 401(k) plan has received no company contributions for several years, offering a "pedestrian" plan to its employees. However, the company provides stock options to all employees, which have led to substantial gains for many, potentially offsetting the lack of traditional 401(k) matching.

feed.title: Employee activism gives life to tech union organizing
feed.summary: The rise of employee activism in the tech sector significantly correlates with increased union organizing efforts. Unlike traditional unionization focused on wages, these tech unions prioritize social issues and values alignment, as seen with groups like the Alphabet Workers Union. While organizing highly skilled workers remains challenging, experts believe a shift in professional identity perception and a broader trend of political activism moving into corporations are driving this change, despite typical corporate resistance to unionization.

feed.title: Comscore Snapshots: online retail in EU5 Region
feed.summary: Comscore's snapshot highlights trends in online retail within the EU5 region (France, Germany, Italy, Spain, and the UK) in late 2020. The retail category holds the fifth position among the top 20 most visited media categories, with over 200 million unique visitors in November 2020. While digital giants dominate, brick-and-mortar retailers are also gaining significant market share, and consumer electronics and apparel attracted the most visitors.

feed.title: Toyota president disappointed by comments from Tokyo 2020 chief Mori
feed.summary: Toyota Motor Corp.'s President Akio Toyoda expressed disappointment over recent comments made by Tokyo 2020 Olympics President Yoshiro Mori, stating they contradicted the values Toyota supports as a top Olympic partner. Toyota, like other sponsors, emphasized its commitment to an inclusive society and gender equality, indicating that Mori's remarks about women talking too much were unacceptable. The company felt it could not remain silent on the issue.

feed.title: Andy Jassy, Amazon’s next CEO, is a climate question mark
feed.summary: Andy Jassy, Amazon's incoming CEO, faces scrutiny over his environmental record, particularly during his leadership of Amazon Web Services (AWS). While Amazon has set ambitious renewable energy goals, critics point to a period of stalled green initiatives at AWS and Jassy's active promotion of cloud services to the oil and gas industry, raising concerns among climate advocates and employees about his commitment to climate action.

feed.title: What If GameStop Actually Thrives After the Gamestonk Saga?
feed.summary: This article explores the possibility of GameStop thriving after the "Gamestonk" saga, thanks to internet entrepreneur Ryan Cohen, co-founder of Chewy. Cohen, who has invested significantly in GameStop and is now on its board, is spearheading a digital transformation aimed at making the company an e-commerce powerhouse for gaming and electronics. His strategy involves applying lessons from Amazon and hiring key talent from both Amazon and Chewy.

feed.title: Exclusive: For Streaming Value, Amazon Prime Video Offers Best Bang for Consumers’ Buck, Report Says
feed.summary: A new Reelgood report, shared exclusively with Morning Consult, indicates that Amazon Prime Video offers the best value for money among streaming services, providing the most movies and TV shows per dollar. While Prime Video excels in quantity per dollar, Netflix leads in original series content, and Disney+ boasts the highest percentage of exclusive content. The report helps consumers navigate the crowded streaming landscape based on budget and content preferences.

feed.title: Amazon questioned over contract with company that offered ‘real-time Uighur warnings’
feed.summary: Senators Robert Menendez and Marco Rubio are questioning Amazon over a reported contract with Dahua, a Chinese security camera company, due to concerns about its technology's ability to identify and track Uighurs. Dahua is on the Commerce Department's entity list for ties to human rights abuses against Uighurs and its software reportedly offered "real-time warnings for non-local Uighurs." Amazon's potential purchase of thermal imaging cameras from Dahua, estimated at $10 million, has raised questions about its adherence to U.S. government guidance regarding such companies.

feed.title: Huami Corp Invests $5 Million in Hyperfine Research D Round to Support Accelerated Application of Disruptive MRI Technology
feed.summary: Huami Corp. (NYSE: HMI) has invested $5 million in Hyperfine Research's D round of funding to support the accelerated application of Hyperfine's disruptive portable MRI technology. Huami believes its expertise in miniaturization engineering aligns with Hyperfine's mission to make MRI imaging more accessible and affordable. Hyperfine Research's Swoop Portable MR Imaging System received FDA 510(K) clearance in August 2020.

feed.title: Introducing your 2021 Marketo Engage Champions!
feed.summary: Adobe has announced its 2021 Marketo Engage Champions, marking the 10th anniversary of the program. This elite group comprises Marketo Engage experts and advocates who share their knowledge, support others, and provide feedback to Adobe's product teams. Champions benefit from career development, speaking opportunities, and connections within the Marketo Engage community.

feed.title: Amazon seller blasts the company's forced arbitration policy in congressional hearing on antitrust
feed.summary: During a congressional hearing on antitrust, an Amazon seller, Jacob Weiss, criticized the company's forced arbitration policy, claiming it prevents fair resolution for small businesses. He highlighted the high costs and limited options for sellers to dispute issues with Amazon, asserting that the system is rigged against them. While House Democrats advocate for eliminating such clauses to strengthen antitrust enforcement, Republicans, like Rep. Ken Buck, express reservations, citing potential unintended consequences.

feed.title: After Months of Lying Low, Alibaba Founder Jack Ma Spotted Playing Golf in Eastern China
feed.summary: Alibaba co-founder Jack Ma has been spotted playing golf in Hainan, eastern China, marking his first public appearance in months. This sighting comes amid increased scrutiny of his companies, Alibaba and Ant Group, by Chinese regulators. Ma's absence from public view followed critical comments he made about China's financial regulator, leading to the cancellation of Ant Group's flotation and an investigation into Alibaba's alleged anti-competition practices.

feed.title: Valentine's Day Gifts For Relationships at Any Stage
feed.summary: This article offers Valentine's Day gift ideas suitable for relationships at various stages, from new romances to long-term partnerships. It emphasizes that thoughtful gifts, regardless of size, can convey affection and provides a diverse list ranging from Mixtiles and scented candles to luxury items and gaming consoles. The author suggests gifts that cater to different preferences and situations, like cozy loungewear for a home-based celebration.

feed.title: All Market’s Vita Coco Boosted caffeinated coconut water – Product Launch
feed.summary: All Market has launched Vita Coco Boosted, a caffeinated version of its coconut water, in the US. This new product contains 40mg of caffeine from tea and MCTs, packaged in three flavors. It is positioned as an energizing drink without the jitters, available through Amazon, Walmart, and Wholefoods.

feed.title: Amazon: Delivering a Complex Class-A Facility in Less Than a Year
feed.summary: Prologis successfully delivered a complex Class-A fulfillment center for Amazon in Tracy, Northern California, in less than a year. This facility was designed to accommodate Amazon's Kiva robotics system, featuring four 10-foot floors and three mezzanine levels, requiring an unusually high clearance of 40 feet. The project was completed just in time for Cyber Monday, significantly reducing fulfillment cycle times for Amazon.

feed.title: Corsair K60 RGB Pro keyboard review
feed.summary: The Corsair K60 RGB Pro is a budget-friendly gaming keyboard that offers premium features like a sleek aluminum design, per-key RGB lighting, and useful iCue software. While it utilizes new Cherry Viola mechanical switches that are similar to MX Reds, they may not provide the same satisfying typing experience for everyone, with some users experiencing issues like accidental double-presses due to the low actuation distance. Despite this, the keyboard is highly recommended for gamers due to its performance, solid build, and attractive price point, especially if opting for the SE model with a wrist rest.

feed.title: United Natural Foods, Inc. Reopens CUB® Banner Store in Minneapolis
feed.summary: United Natural Foods, Inc. (UNFI) has reopened its CUB® banner store on Lake Street in Minneapolis after it was forced to close due to significant damage in 2020. The renovated store features enlarged specialty areas and new community-suggested amenities, including an expanded deli, a global foods market, and a freshly made tortillas station. This reopening marks a significant step for CUB, showcasing its commitment to the community by rebuilding with enhanced offerings and services.

feed.title: SuperMicro server spy chip story returns, with no more proof than before
feed.summary: Bloomberg has revisited its 2018 report alleging Chinese spy chips in Super Micro server hardware used by Apple, Amazon, and the US government, but the new story offers no additional corroborating evidence. Apple, Amazon, SuperMicro, and several US federal agencies previously denied the original claims, with Apple CEO Tim Cook criticizing Bloomberg's reporting. Despite the continued denials and lack of proof, Bloomberg stands by its controversial story.

feed.title: Qualcomm objects to Nvidia’s $40 billion Arm acquisition
feed.summary: Qualcomm has formally expressed its opposition to Nvidia's proposed $40 billion acquisition of Arm to regulators worldwide, including the FTC, European Commission, and UK's Competition and Markets Authority. The U.S. chipmaker fears Nvidia could become a gatekeeper of Arm's technology, restricting access for other chipmakers. Regulators are now in a "second phase" of investigation, seeking more information from the involved companies.

feed.title: Years later, Bloomberg doubles down on disputed Supermicro supply chain hack story
feed.summary: Bloomberg has published a new article, years after its initial disputed report, to double down on its claims that the Chinese state infiltrated Supermicro to plant secret chips on its motherboards. While Supermicro and other companies involved continue to deny these allegations, the new report offers additional context and quotes, alleging wider historical tampering and government investigations. Despite the ongoing denials and lack of official government confirmation, Bloomberg stands by its investigative journalism.

feed.title: Tobi vs. Goliath: How Shopify is bracing for a looming battle with Amazon
feed.summary: Shopify, under the leadership of co-founder and CEO Tobi Lutke, has seen significant growth, surpassing Royal Bank of Canada in market capitalization due to a surge in e-commerce during the pandemic. Despite this success, Lutke expresses dissatisfaction with Shopify's current platform, striving for continuous improvement and likening the company's role to "arming the rebels" against Amazon's "empire." The article highlights Shopify's strategic shift to adapt to a 2030 e-commerce landscape, its challenges in retaining key executives and managing remote work culture, and its potential weaknesses like a small patent portfolio, as it prepares for intensified competition with Amazon.

feed.title: Spotify set for uphill battle in South Korea
feed.summary: Spotify recently launched its service in South Korea, a market already dominated by local music streaming services like Melon, Genie Music, and Flo. Despite its global presence, Spotify faces challenges including a lack of content from major Korean artists due to an unconfirmed deal with Kakao M, and increasing competition from both established local players bolstering their services and new contenders like Clubhouse. The company hopes to succeed by offering tailored playlists, a vast catalog, and launching podcast services, but its success is not guaranteed in this competitive landscape.

feed.title: The Fight for Fifteen at an Orlando McDonald’s
feed.summary: This article details the struggles of Cristian Cardona, a McDonald's shift manager in Orlando, and his co-workers during the COVID-19 pandemic, highlighting their fight for better wages and working conditions. It connects their experiences to the nationwide "Fight for Fifteen" movement, emphasizing how corporations like McDonald's capitalized on the pandemic while many employees faced inadequate protection and stagnant pay. The piece argues that the pandemic exposed the critical role of low-wage "essential" workers and the disconnect between their perceived value and their compensation.

feed.title: Sonos One vs Apple HomePod: Which smart speaker should you buy?
feed.summary: This article compares the Sonos One and Apple HomePod smart speakers to help consumers decide which one to purchase. It evaluates them based on price, multi-room system compatibility, music service support, control methods, and loudness. The Sonos One typically comes out ahead for versatility and value, while the HomePod is preferred for Apple users with larger rooms and deeper pockets.

feed.title: BKLC Share Price & Live Chart | BNY Mellon US Large Cap Core Equity ETF
feed.summary: The BNY Mellon US Large Cap Core Equity ETF (BKLC) offers investors exposure to US large-cap equities, tracking the S&P 500 TR USD index. The article provides live share price data, historical performance, technical analysis, and key metrics such as its current price of 140.58, a 1.2% dividend yield, and a 0.00% expense ratio. It also lists top holdings including NVIDIA, Apple, and Microsoft.

feed.title: Amazon acquires Selz, a Shopify competitor that helps small businesses build online stores
feed.summary: Amazon has quietly acquired Selz, an Australian company that provides tools for small businesses to build online stores, on January 15. This move positions Amazon to compete more directly with e-commerce enablers like Shopify, especially as its third-party marketplace continues to grow significantly. Selz will continue to operate, with its CEO stating that nothing is changing for its existing customers.

feed.title: SL Green Sells Stake in Tower 46 to Brookfield Asset Management for $275M
feed.summary: SL Green Realty Corp. has agreed to sell its 25 percent stake in office building Tower 46 to Brookfield Asset Management for $275 million. This deal, expected to close in Q1, includes commercial condominium units and a retail space in the Midtown building. The sale is part of SL Green's strategy to divest non-core assets and reinvest capital into its share repurchase program, demonstrating the resiliency of the Manhattan office market.

feed.title: Winter storm temporarily closes hundreds of Walmart stores and Amazon facilities, snarling delivery
feed.summary: A severe winter storm caused the temporary closure of hundreds of Walmart stores and Amazon facilities across several states, particularly impacting Texas and the Southeast. This led to significant disruptions in package delivery services from FedEx and UPS, which also closed major shipping hubs. Millions in Texas experienced power outages due to the extreme weather conditions.

feed.title: Korean E-Commerce Giant Coupang’s IPO Filing Sheds Light on a Nagging Issue: Fakes
feed.summary: Coupang, dubbed the "Amazon of South Korea," has filed for an initial public offering, revealing strong growth and narrowing losses, with an expected valuation of over $50 billion. However, its SEC filing also highlights a significant challenge: the pervasive issue of counterfeit products on its platform. This problem is similar to those faced by other major e-commerce platforms and poses a risk to Coupang's reputation and customer trust.

feed.title: Oracle revives protest of $10B JEDI war cloud contract: Report
feed.summary: Oracle is requesting the U.S. Supreme Court re-examine its challenge to the $10 billion Joint Enterprise Defense Infrastructure (JEDI) cloud contract, initially awarded to Microsoft in 2019. The company argues the bidding process was flawed with conflicts of interest and restricted competition. This renewed challenge comes after previous attempts by Oracle and Amazon to contest the contract award.

feed.title: An Interview With Twilio CEO Jeff Lawson
feed.summary: This article features an interview with Twilio CEO Jeff Lawson, author of "Ask Your Developer," discussing how companies can harness the power of software developers to win in the 21st century. Lawson emphasizes the "build or die" philosophy, where software development is a core competency for modern businesses and highlights the importance of organizing companies into small, autonomous teams and empowering developers to solve business problems rather than just implementing prescribed solutions. The interview also touches on Twilio's own practices, including requiring all employees to build an app, and the company's focus on markets outside of China.

feed.title: Amazon documents reveal company’s secret strategy to dodge India’s regulators
feed.summary: Internal Amazon documents reveal the company's strategy to navigate and "test the boundaries" of India's foreign direct investment regulations, which aim to protect small retailers. The documents show Amazon favored a small group of sellers, some with indirect equity ties to Amazon, accounting for a disproportionately large share of sales, and helped them secure exclusive deals and subsidized fees. This practice allowed Amazon to circumvent rules prohibiting direct sales and inventory ownership by e-commerce platforms, contradicting its public statements about fair competition and compliance with Indian law.

feed.title: Another giant podcast buy: iHeartMedia to acquire Triton Digital for $230m
feed.summary: iHeartMedia has announced its agreement to acquire Triton Digital for $230 million, a move that further consolidates its position in the audio advertising technology and podcasting space. Triton Digital is an Advertising Technology SaaS platform specializing in audio streaming, podcasting, and metrics, operating in over 50 countries. This acquisition follows iHeartMedia's recent purchase of Voxnest and other investments, highlighting a trend of significant podcast and audio tech buyouts in the industry.

feed.title: 20 more companies from around the globe join The Climate Pledge
feed.summary: Amazon and Global Optimism announced that 20 new companies have joined The Climate Pledge, bringing the total number of signatories to 53 across various industries and countries. These companies commit to achieving net-zero carbon emissions by 2040, a decade ahead of the Paris Agreement's goal. The new members include diverse businesses like ACCIONA, IBM, Ørsted, and UPM, all dedicated to implementing decarbonization strategies and reducing their environmental impact.

feed.title: What’s Changed: 2021 Gartner Magic Quadrant for Analytics and Business Intelligence Platforms
feed.summary: Solutions Review highlights the key changes and analyses the 2021 Gartner Magic Quadrant for Analytics and Business Intelligence Platforms. The report notes increased adoption, cloud deployments, and a shift in differentiation towards augmented analytics. It details the positions of leading vendors like Microsoft, Tableau, and Qlik, alongside challengers and visionaries, while also outlining new entrants and those removed from the quadrant.

feed.title: Clean200 Surges Ahead of MSCI ACWI Global Index, Leaves Index of Dirty Energy Companies in the Dust
feed.summary: The seventh update of the Carbon Clean 200 report by As You Sow and Corporate Knights reveals that Clean200 companies, focused on clean energy, significantly outperformed the MSCI ACWI broad market index and the MSCI ACWI/Energy Index of fossil fuel companies. Since its inception on July 1, 2016, through January 31, 2021, the Clean200 generated a total return of 113.41%, compared to 78.67% for the broad market and a -13.83% for fossil fuel companies. This report highlights the strong financial performance of sustainable solutions and the market's growing confidence in the clean energy sector.

feed.title: Exclusive: How Oracle Sells Repression in China
feed.summary: Oracle has aggressively marketed its data analytics software to Chinese police and security forces, including those in Xinjiang, for surveillance and predictive policing, despite concerns about human rights and national security implications. This practice clashes with the company's stated values and its bid for TikTok, which aimed to prevent data access by Chinese authorities. Oracle representatives claim these were "aspirational business development ideas" and not actual implementations, but internal documents and former employees suggest otherwise.

feed.title: Minnesota-Based Sun Country Airlines Has Filed to Go Public
feed.summary: Sun Country Airlines, a Minnesota-based discount airline, has filed for a $100 million IPO on the Nasdaq under the ticker symbol "SNCY." Despite the challenges of the COVID-19 pandemic, the airline's flexible business model, including cargo services for Amazon, has allowed it to navigate the downturn effectively. Apollo Global Management will retain majority voting power after the IPO, with Barclays, Deutsche Bank, and Morgan Stanley underwriting the offering.

feed.title: AgEagle Aerial Systems - Class Action Investigation On
feed.summary: Kehoe Law Firm, P.C. is investigating potential securities fraud claims against AgEagle Aerial Systems Inc. following a Bonitas Research report. The report alleges AgEagle was a "pump & dump scheme" and questions the existence of a "major e-commerce customer" or drone technology. This news led to a significant drop in AgEagle's stock price, prompting the firm to encourage investors with losses greater than $50,000 to contact them.

feed.title: [NEWS IN FOCUS] New York listing for Coupang not as odd as it might seem
feed.summary: Coupang, the Korean e-commerce giant, has opted to list on the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE) rather than Nasdaq or the Korea Exchange, a decision driven by its U.S.-incorporated entity and significant non-Korean shareholding. Industry experts suggest this move aims to leverage the NYSE's greater liquidity and access to global institutional investors, while also showcasing confidence in its improving financial performance and sidestepping stringent Korean regulations. The company also introduced a dual-class share structure to secure founder control, a system not permitted in Korea.

feed.title: P2P specialist BuyerQuest is acquired by ODP, a provider of business services and technology
feed.summary: The ODP Corporation, parent company of Office Depot, has acquired BuyerQuest Holdings Inc., a leading e-procurement and procure-to-pay (P2P) software provider. This acquisition aims to accelerate ODP's technology development and integrate e-commerce, P2P, and supply chain platforms, enhancing its B2B digital strategy. The move follows ODP's hiring of Prentis Wilson, formerly of Amazon Business, to lead its digital transformation efforts.

feed.title: Problems at AgEagle Aerial Systems (UAVS)
feed.summary: The article exposes significant issues at AgEagle Aerial Systems (UAVS), challenging its self-proclaimed status as a drone technology pioneer. It reveals the company lacks patents, has minimal R&D spending, and uses an external part-time CTO, while an alleged Amazon partnership has been denied by Amazon. The piece also highlights the problematic management history and concerning financial practices involving Alpha Capital Anstalt, which has a history of involvement in pump-and-dump schemes.

feed.title: What is CIEM and why should CISOs care?
feed.summary: Cloud Infrastructure Entitlement Management (CIEM) is a new category of identity-centric SaaS solutions focused on managing cloud access risk within hybrid and multi-cloud IaaS environments through administration-time controls. Unlike SIEM, CIEM uses analytics and machine learning to detect anomalies in account entitlements, such as privilege accumulation and dormant entitlements, and aims to remediate and enforce least privilege approaches. CISOs should care about CIEM because it addresses the increasing security complexities and permissions gaps in multi-cloud environments, particularly concerning inactive identities, overpermissioned active identities, cross-account access vulnerabilities, and anomalous behavior among machine identities.

feed.title: AC Brands' Emma Koehler Talks Sumo Citrus® Merchandising
feed.summary: Emma Koehler, Assistant Brand Manager at AC Brands, shares merchandising strategies for Sumo Citrus to maximize sales, emphasizing eye-catching displays in high-traffic areas and separation from other citrus varieties. She highlights that Sumo Citrus, known for its unique size and shape and high Vitamin C content, has been on the market for 10 seasons. The company aims for consumers to discover this unique product during the winter months when health is a priority.

feed.title: Wayfair to Open New Fulfillment Center in Romeoville
iShares S&P 500 Growth ETF Trade Ideas — AMEX:IVW
feed.summary: This article compiles trade ideas and market sentiment analysis related to the iShares S&P 500 Growth ETF (AMEX: IVW). Several contributors discuss the ongoing debate and rotation between growth and value stocks, analyzing factors like interest rates, economic conditions, and fundamental valuations. The articles explore various market sentiment indicators over short, medium, and long terms, providing perspectives on potential market shifts and risk appetite.

feed.title: One year ago: A New York City Toy Fair, and hints of coming horrors
feed.summary: The author recounts his experience at the New York City Toy Fair in February 2020, just before the COVID-19 pandemic fully gripped the U.S. He describes the initial signs of unease about the virus amidst the bustling event and how his perception changed during his trip back to Providence. The article reflects on the contrast between the carefree atmosphere of the fair and the subsequent global impact of the pandemic.

feed.title: The Bill and Melinda Gates Foundation Sells Its Alibaba Shares
feed.summary: The Bill and Melinda Gates Foundation sold its entire stake in Chinese e-commerce giant Alibaba, earning $21.13 million from the sale. Simultaneously, the foundation also divested its shares in Uber and reduced its holdings in Apple and Amazon, while significantly increasing its investment in the software company Schrodinger. These changes reflect a notable shift in the foundation's investment portfolio.

feed.title: SHAREHOLDER ALERT: AgEagle Aerial Systems Inc. Investigated
feed.summary: Block & Leviton LLP is investigating AgEagle Aerial Systems Inc. (NYSE: UAVS) for potential securities law violations following a Bonitas Research report. The report alleges AgEagle is a "pump & dump scheme" that used rumors of a partnership with Amazon, which Amazon later denied. This led to a significant drop in AgEagle's stock price, causing hundreds of millions in lost market capitalization.

feed.title: EXCLUSIVE Discount retailer Dollar General takes steps to find possible CEO successor -sources
feed.summary: Dollar General Corp is reportedly looking for a potential successor to CEO Todd Vasos, even though Vasos has not indicated plans to step down. The discount retailer has begun discussions with headhunting firms and is considering both internal candidates, like COO Jeff Owen, and external candidates. This move is framed by the company as "merely good governance" for succession planning, despite Vasos's contract renewing automatically.

feed.title: Valvoline Revs Up Convenience with Gear Oil in Stand-Up Pouches
feed.summary: Valvoline has introduced a new stand-up pouch, named FlexFill™, for its synthetic gear oil, designed to make dispensing easier for DIY-ers and reduce product waste. This flexible packaging replaces traditional rigid HDPE bottles, addressing consumer challenges related to accessibility under cars and oil remaining in containers. The 32-oz FlexFill pouch is 60% lighter than the previous bottle, contributing to a more convenient and potentially less wasteful automotive maintenance experience.

feed.title: Apple buys a company every three to four weeks
feed.summary: Apple CEO Tim Cook revealed that the company has acquired approximately 100 companies over the past six years, averaging one acquisition every three to four weeks. These acquisitions are primarily focused on gaining technology and talent, helping Apple to integrate innovations into its products and expand into areas like AI, self-driving technology, and payments. Despite its rapid acquisition pace, Apple tends to buy smaller firms and has been more financially restrained in its purchases compared to other big tech companies.

feed.title: USPS selects Oshkosh Defense to build greener mail truck
feed.summary: The USPS has chosen Oshkosh Defense to build its next-generation mail delivery vehicles as part of a multi-billion dollar, 10-year effort to modernize its fleet and incorporate more environmentally friendly options. Oshkosh Defense will produce 50,000 to 165,000 Next Generation Delivery Vehicles, which will include a mix of electric and gasoline-powered options, with the latter designed for future electric retrofitting. This decision is a setback for electric vehicle startup Workhorse Group, whose shares dropped significantly after its all-electric bid was passed over.

feed.title: How Capital One Moved Its Data Analytics to the Cloud
feed.summary: Capital One, a leader in technology-driven banking, successfully transitioned its data analytics from on-premise data centers to the public cloud, becoming the first U.S. bank to do so in 2020. This move leveraged the Snowflake Data Cloud to enhance scalability, support thousands of analysts, and meet stringent security and compliance requirements. The adoption of the cloud platform has enabled Capital One to improve customer experiences, control costs through consumption-based pricing, and gain real-time access to larger and more varied datasets.

feed.title: Ocean Cargo Snarls Force Global Commerce to Look Outside the Box
feed.summary: An ongoing surge in global shipping costs is forcing manufacturers and their customers to seek alternative solutions, considering purchasing their own containers and chartering vessels. This supply chain crunch, fueled by increased demand and container scarcity, threatens to impede economic recovery and could eventually lead to higher consumer prices. Despite carriers' efforts to meet demand, the situation remains challenging, with some economists monitoring the potential for inflation.

feed.title: Security top of mind for digital transformation projects
feed.summary: A new ASUG and Onapsis survey reveals that security is a top concern for SAP customers undertaking S/4HANA migrations and other digital transformation projects, especially when moving to the cloud. The survey indicated that these modernization projects have not been significantly slowed by the COVID-19 pandemic. While cloud providers handle some security aspects, organizations remain responsible for data security and for addressing the unique complexities and vulnerabilities of SAP applications in a hybrid IT environment.

feed.title: Electronics chain Fry’s is closing for good
feed.summary: Electronics retailer Fry's Electronics, known for its themed stores and wide selection of tech parts, has announced its permanent closure due to the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic. The 36-year-old chain, which had 31 stores primarily on the West Coast, was already struggling against online competition and strong rivals like Best Buy and Amazon. Its closure marks the end of an era for many loyal customers and tech enthusiasts.

feed.title: IonQ to Go Public Via SPAC?
feed.summary: IonQ, a Maryland-based pure-play quantum computing company, is reportedly in talks to go public through a merger with SPAC DMY Technology Group Inc. The deal is potentially worth around $2 billion and would make IonQ the first U.S.-based quantum company to go public this way. Investors like Silver Lake, MSD Partners, Bill Gates’ Breakthrough Energy, and Hyundai Motor are said to be involved in the potential transaction, with additional equity being raised from institutional investors.

feed.title: LG opens WebOS TV platform to third-party TV manufacturers
feed.summary: LG Electronics has announced that its webOS TV platform will now be available to other TV brand partners, making it no longer exclusive to LG products. This move follows a significant overhaul of webOS, showcased at CES 2021, and has already garnered commitments from approximately twenty TV manufacturers globally. LG aims to expand the webOS ecosystem by partnering with major streaming services and leveraging its acquisition of Alphonso Inc. for enhanced user experience.

feed.title: Fry's Electronics shuts down all stores, including Austin
feed.summary: Fry's Electronics, a notable chain for tech enthusiasts, has announced the permanent closure of all its stores, including the Austin location. The company cited the COVID-19 pandemic as making continued operations impossible, though it was already facing significant competition from online retailers and big box stores like Best Buy and Amazon. Fry's Electronics Inc. has ceased operations and will begin winding down locations immediately, asking customers to pick up electronics being repaired.

feed.title: Fry's Closes: 31 Computer and Electronics Stores Shut Down -
feed.summary: After nearly 36 years in business, Fry's Electronics has announced the permanent shutdown of its 31 computer and electronics stores across nine states. The company cited changes in the retail industry and challenges from the COVID-19 pandemic, alongside intense competition from e-commerce giants like Amazon and Best Buy, as reasons for its closure. Fry's struggled with online sales, launching them years after competitors, and experienced business difficulties even before the pandemic hit.

feed.title: Fight for $15: US retailer Costco raises its hourly wage to $16
feed.summary: Costco Wholesale Corp is increasing its minimum wage for hourly staff to $16, exceeding competitors Amazon and Target, and more than double the federal minimum wage. This move aligns with US President Joe Biden's plan to raise the minimum wage to $15 by 2025 and follows similar action by Walmart. The decision was announced by Costco's CEO Craig Jelinek at a Senate Budget Committee hearing, emphasizing the company's desire to retain employees, amidst broader debate on the federal minimum wage increase and its inclusion in the COVID-19 relief bill.

feed.title: AT&T sells part of DirecTV to buyout firm TPG
feed.summary: AT&T has agreed to sell a 30% stake in its struggling DirecTV unit to private-equity firm TPG for $1.8 billion, creating a new entity that will jointly run DirecTV and AT&T TV services in the U.S. AT&T will retain a 70% share and receive $7.8 billion in cash to help pay down its debts, reflecting a significant devaluation from its 2015 acquisition of DirecTV. This move is part of AT&T's strategy to divest from non-core assets amidst a "cord-cutting" trend and focus on its HBO Max streaming service.

feed.title: See new renderings of PENN 15 designed by Foster + Partners
feed.summary: New renderings for PENN 15, designed by Foster + Partners, have been revealed, showcasing a 1,200-foot tower with different-sized glass containers and 27 landscaped outdoor spaces. This latest design by Vornado Realty Trust is part of the larger PENN District redevelopment, aiming to replace the former Hotel Pennsylvania and add nearly 3 million square feet of rental space. The project features a side core design for flexible office space, though its "Amazon-eco-Helix inspired" aesthetic and focus on green solutions raise questions in a post-COVID context.

feed.title: Costco is raising its minimum wage to $16 an hour for its U.S. store workers
feed.summary: Costco is increasing its minimum hourly wage for U.S. store workers to $16, surpassing competitors like Amazon, Walmart, and Best Buy. This decision comes as Congress debates a federal minimum wage increase to $15 an hour. The company previously instituted a $2 an hour premium during the pandemic, and this new raise converts part of that temporary premium into a permanent increase.

feed.title: Costco raises minimum wage to $16 per hour
feed.summary: Costco Wholesale Corp announced it will increase its minimum wage for hourly staff to $16 per hour, effective next week. This new wage is a dollar higher than what its major competitors, including Walmart, Amazon, and Target, currently pay their hourly employees. The move positions Costco with a leading minimum wage among large retailers.

feed.title: Costco lifts minimum wage above Amazon or Target to $16 per hour
feed.summary: Costco Wholesale Corp is raising its minimum wage for hourly staff to $16, surpassing competitors Amazon and Target. The decision was announced by CEO Craig Jelinek at a U.S. Senate Budget Committee hearing and comes as President Joe Biden aims to increase the federal minimum wage to $15 by 2025. Costco, which employs over 180,000 people in the U.S., had previously raised its minimum wage to $15 in 2019 and offered premium pay during the COVID-19 pandemic.

feed.title: Jeff Bezos’ Blue Origin delays New Glenn debut to late 2022 - Science News
feed.summary: Jeff Bezos' Blue Origin has delayed the maiden launch of its New Glenn rocket to the fourth quarter of 2022. This postponement follows Blue Origin's failure to secure a contract for the National Security Space Launch Phase 2, losing out to SpaceX and United Launch Alliance. Blue Origin is now focusing on winning a NASA lunar lander contract, seen as crucial for its profitability and standing as a major space partner.

feed.title: Deals: Apple AirPods Pro $189, $100 off SanDisk Portable SSD
feed.summary: Amazon-owned Woot has reissued a flash deal on Apple AirPods Pro, bringing their price down to $189 (a $60 discount) for one day only. Separately, a 1TB SanDisk Extreme Portable SSD is also on sale for $149.99, saving buyers $100 off the MSRP. Various other Apple products, such as Magic Keyboards, MacBook Pros, and iMacs, are also available at discounted prices through AppleInsider and authorized resellers.

feed.title: Seattle Travel Guide: Vacation + Trip Ideas
feed.summary: Seattle, a major hub in the Pacific Northwest, offers a blend of city life and natural beauty with its waterfronts and mountains. Known for its exceptional coffee, vibrant music scene, and fresh seafood, the city also boasts popular attractions like Pike Place Market and the Space Needle. The guide provides insights into the best time to visit, transportation, top hotels, restaurants, shopping, and distinct neighborhoods, making it ideal for travelers.

feed.title: Fall 21′ Lift: Calvin Comes to Kohl’s
feed.summary: Kohl's will begin selling Calvin Klein men’s underwear, women’s intimates, and loungewear in fall 2021, expanding its national brand assortment. This move, which includes dedicated in-store experiences and an expanded online selection, is part of Kohl's strategy to become a stronger destination for active and casual apparel. The retailer aims to increase active and outdoor categories to 30% of its total volume, while also launching Sephora shops within its stores and culling underperforming brands.

feed.title: Vizio Aims for Some of That Roku-like Magic with IPO
feed.summary: Vizio is pursuing an initial public offering, positioning itself as a technology company akin to Roku by leveraging its SmartCast operating system and "Platform+" business. The company reported a 20% increase in smart TV shipments and 11% revenue growth in 2020, with its SmartCast OS gathering 12.2 million active users. Vizio aims for its Platform+ segment, focused on advertising and data sales, to drive future margin growth and financial performance, similar to how Roku's platform revenues have outperformed hardware sales.

feed.title: Kohl's says it added 2 million new customers in 2020, thanks to Amazon
feed.summary: Kohl's announced it gained 2 million new customers in 2020, attributing this growth primarily to its Amazon returns service. The company noted that about a third of these new customers are millennials and sees the partnership as accretive to both sales and profit. This news comes as Kohl's faces pressure from activist investors skeptical of the Amazon collaboration's impact on sales.

feed.title: As Amazon Air Expands, Report Sees Potential Challenge to UPS, FedEx and USPS
feed.summary: A new report suggests Amazon Air's significant growth could soon position it as a direct competitor to major air and ground carriers like FedEx, UPS, and the U.S. Postal Service. Amazon Air is rapidly expanding its flight schedule and fleet, with plans to exceed 160 flights per day, and is expected to test expanded business-to-consumer delivery services by the 2021 holiday season. This expansion indicates Amazon's potential to become a third-party logistics provider, especially for business-to-consumer shipping, leveraging its existing supply chain.

feed.title: Amazon apologises to Indian viewers over Tandav controversy
feed.summary: Amazon has issued an unconditional apology to Indian viewers after its political drama series "Tandav" allegedly offended Hindu religious beliefs. The streaming service stated that it deeply regrets that certain scenes were considered objectionable and affirmed its commitment to developing content while respecting audience diversity. This apology follows police complaints and court cases against the show in several Indian states and comes as Amazon faces scrutiny in India over its business practices.

feed.title: Coupang set to raise $3.6 bn via US listing; eyes throne as platform giant
feed.summary: Coupang, South Korea's e-commerce giant, plans to raise up to $3.6 billion through its US IPO, potentially reaching a market cap of $51 billion. The company aims to use the proceeds to expand into logistics, content, and food delivery, challenging Korean platform leaders Naver and Kakao. Founder Kim Bom-suk will maintain management control through a dual-class share structure, holding 76.7% of voting interests post-IPO.
feed.source: The Korea Economic Daily Global Edition
feed.source_domain: The Korea Economic Daily Global Edition

feed.title: FedEx pledges $2 billion toward carbon-neutral operations by 2040, aims for all-electric fleet
feed.summary: FedEx announced a significant commitment of at least $2 billion towards sustainable energy initiatives, with a goal to achieve carbon-neutral operations by 2040. This plan includes transitioning its entire parcel pickup and delivery fleet to electric vehicles and investing $100 million in Yale University for carbon capture research. The move aligns FedEx with other major corporations setting ambitious sustainability targets.

feed.title: The Social-Tracking BUZZ ETF: What It Is, What It Isn't
feed.summary: The article introduces the VanEck Vectors Social Sentiment ETF (BUZZ), explaining how it tracks positive social media sentiment for large-cap stocks using AI. It clarifies that BUZZ is a sentiment tracker for established companies, not a replication of the r/WallStreetBets phenomenon, emphasizing the fund's broader approach to social sentiment and its previous iteration as the Sprott Buzz Social Media Insights ETF. The article also delves into the controversy surrounding Dave Portnoy's involvement with the index provider and the marketing of the ETF.

feed.title: What’s Changed: 2021 Gartner Magic Quadrant for Data Science and Machine Learning Platforms
feed.summary: This article analyzes Gartner's 2021 Magic Quadrant for Data Science and Machine Learning Platforms, highlighting new additions and key differentiators among vendors. It details the leaders, challengers, and visionaries in the market, discussing their strengths and how the market is evolving with increased capabilities and the convergence with analytics and business intelligence.

feed.title: COVID and the Outlook for Canadian Women at Risk of Disruption
feed.summary: This article discusses the disproportionate impact of COVID-19 on Canadian women, particularly those in low-skilled service jobs, with nearly half a million not yet returned to work by January. It highlights that women bore the brunt of job losses due to their majority presence in affected industries and warns of long-term unemployment risks and skills erosion. The authors emphasize the need for government and business leaders to invest in retraining and reskilling programs, alongside support for affordable childcare, to ensure an inclusive economic recovery.

feed.title: Whole Foods, UNFI Extend Distribution Partnership
feed.summary: United Natural Foods Inc. (UNFI) and Whole Foods Market have extended their primary wholesale grocery distribution agreement until September 27, 2027. Both companies highlighted their close collaboration during the COVID-19 pandemic to meet consumer demand for healthy foods. This extension allows them to continue focusing on the evolving food supply chain and the growing demand for natural and organic products.

feed.title: Charting a volatile March start: S&P 500, Nasdaq reach key technical tests
feed.summary: U.S. stocks experienced a volatile start to March, with the S&P 500 retesting its 50-day moving average at 3,820 and the Nasdaq Composite finding support at its 2020 peak of 12,973. The article highlights sector backdrops for various ETFs including IYT, XLF, QQQ, XLV, XLU, XLP, XLE, and XLI, which are still bullish-leaning. This comes as markets are mixed early Thursday, but off session lows.

feed.title: Dow, Bitcoin Cautiously Advance into February Following Stock Market’s Best January in Decades
feed.summary: The Dow and Bitcoin showed cautious movement into February despite a strong January for the stock market. Bullish jobs numbers, defying government shutdown predictions, helped the Dow, while Bitcoin saw a moderate incline driven by a re-filed ETF application and Fidelity's progress on crypto custody, though it remained below a critical support level.

feed.title: Synchrony’s CHRO Explains How COVID-19 Transformed How the Company Works – and Why It’s Encouraging All Employees to Work From Home Even After the Pandemic
feed.summary: Synchrony Financial is making its work-from-home policy permanent for its 16,500 employees, transforming its office structure into "hubs" and reducing real estate square footage by 40%. This decision was driven by employee feedback and a recognition that the pandemic necessitated new approaches to work, including enhanced benefits like childcare reimbursement and mental health support. CHRO DJ Casto explains how this shift aims to improve employee quality of life and leverage cost savings from real estate to invest back into the workforce.

feed.title: United Natural Foods, Inc. Extends its Partnership with Whole Foods Market
feed.summary: United Natural Foods, Inc. (UNFI) announced the extension of its primary grocery distribution agreement with Whole Foods Market until September 27, 2027. This extension strengthens their long-standing partnership, allowing both companies to continue meeting the growing demand for healthy foods, especially amidst the global pandemic. The collaboration is seen as crucial for supporting Whole Foods Market's expanding network of stores and facilities.

feed.title: Urban Outfitters tests paid membership program to boost loyalty
feed.summary: Urban Outfitters is testing a paid membership program called "UP" to boost customer loyalty, drive sales, and gather data. The program offers benefits across all its brands, including free shipping and discounts, and is being piloted in Atlanta and Dallas. This initiative aims to address declining sales post-pandemic and increase engagement in a competitive retail landscape.

feed.title: Everything you need to know about the Coupang IPO
feed.summary: Coupang, South Korea's largest and fastest e-commerce service, held its IPO on March 11, 2021, on the NYSE under the symbol CPNG, raising $4.6 billion. The company, though not yet profitable, focuses on rapid growth and efficiency in the Korean market, with 99.3% of orders delivered within 24 hours. Coupang is expanding its services into areas like advertising, fintech, and food delivery, and its IPO sought capital for investments in AI and robotics to enhance its efficient operations.

feed.title: Ulta Beauty Opens in Herald Square
feed.summary: Ulta Beauty has opened its second and largest Manhattan location at 2 Herald Square, aiming to revitalize the area and create over 100 new jobs. The store features a modern interior, customized displays for major beauty brands, a lofted salon, and dedicated space for events and classes. Ulta is also enhancing its online shopping experience with in-store pickup and the GlamLab AI app, and recently partnered with Loop for package recycling.

feed.title: Can Starbucks Stock Remain Unfazed by the Broad-Market Selloff?
feed.summary: Starbucks (SBUX) stock has shown resilience despite a broader market downturn, with its 80-day moving average containing recent losses. The company is set to hold its annual shareholder meeting, which could lead to analyst upgrades and further boost the stock. While SBUX has a high current price-earnings ratio, its forward P/E is significantly lower, and its options currently suggest low volatility expectations, making it a potential entry point for long-term investors.

feed.title: Entrepreneurs are seeing serious growth through Amazon. Here's how
feed.summary: The article highlights how entrepreneurs are achieving significant growth by partnering with Amazon, specifically through its Delivery Service Partner program. Jacqueline Nelson, a retired registered nurse, successfully operates NW Premier Logistics LLC, delivering thousands of packages weekly in Metro Detroit as an Amazon DSP, demonstrating a pathway to entrepreneurial success.

feed.title: Private equity pours $500M into Saks Fifth Avenue’s e-commerce spinoff
feed.summary: HBC, the parent company of Saks Fifth Avenue, is spinning off the luxury retailer's e-commerce operations into a new standalone company called "Saks" following a $500 million investment from Insight Partners. This move values the new digital entity at $2 billion, with Insight Partners taking a minority stake. Marc Metrick will lead the new e-commerce company, while the 40 physical Saks Fifth Avenue stores will remain wholly owned by HBC and operate separately as "SFA."

feed.title: Saks Fifth Avenue owner spins e-commerce site into separate business
feed.summary: HBC, the owner of Saks Fifth Avenue, is spinning off the luxury department store's e-commerce business, Saks.com, into a separate entity after securing a $500 million investment from venture capital firm Insight Partners. This deal values Saks.com at $2 billion. The physical Saks Fifth Avenue stores will continue as a separate business, SFA, wholly owned by HBC, reflecting the increasing importance of online sales, especially in the luxury sector.

feed.title: Luxury E-commerce Market to See Massive Growth by 2026 |Amazon, Neiman Marcus, Moda Operandi
feed.summary: A new report by HTF Market Intelligence projects massive growth for the global luxury e-commerce market by 2026, with key players including Amazon, Neiman Marcus, and Moda Operandi. The study provides an in-depth analysis of market size, competitive landscape, product types (e.g., bags, watches, jewelry), and applications, alongside regional breakdowns and the impact of COVID-19. It aims to help stakeholders identify growth opportunities and strategies in various micro-markets.

feed.title: Amazon-leased building in Bucks County sells for more than double the price of 2019 sale
feed.summary: An Amazon-leased building in Levittown, Bucks County, was sold to Realty Income Corp. for $21.1 million, more than doubling its 2019 sale price. This transaction highlights the significant value appreciation of industrial properties with long-term Amazon leases. The deal underscores the "lottery ticket" effect a long-term lease with Amazon can have on previously older industrial buildings.

feed.title: Future Group's Kishore Biyani to launch US-based Terra chips in India
feed.summary: Despite ongoing legal battles with Amazon, Kishore Biyani's Future Group is launching US-based Terra chips in India. The company, through its Future Consumer arm, has been working for four years to localize production of the gourmet chips, aiming to reduce costs by 150% and make them more accessible. This move is part of Future Consumer's strategy to become a major player in the snacking sector, with plans to export to other Asian and Middle Eastern countries.

feed.title: Quantum computing startup IonQ announces $2bn SPAC merger
feed.summary: Quantum computing startup IonQ has announced a $2 billion merger with dMY Technology Group, Inc. III, a Special Purpose Acquisition Company (SPAC), making it the first publicly traded quantum computing company. Its shares are expected to trade on the NYSE under "IONQ." This move follows recent trends of data center-related companies utilizing SPACs for quicker market entry.

feed.title: IonQ to become publicly traded pure quantum computing company
feed.summary: Quantum computing firm IonQ Inc. announced its plan to go public through a merger with dMY Technology Group Inc. III, a special-purpose acquisition company, valuing IonQ at approximately $2 billion. Upon closing, IonQ will trade under the symbol "IONQ" and aims to commercialize quantum computing by making its services available via cloud platforms like Amazon Bracket and Microsoft Azure. The company plans to use the $650 million raised to continue research, miniaturize its technology, and develop modular quantum computers.

feed.title: Breaking: Sprouts will plant new distribution center next to Orlando airport
feed.summary: Sprouts Farmers Market is planning to open a new distribution center next to the Orlando airport, with buildout construction estimated to cost $9.3 million. This new facility will support Sprouts' operations in Central Florida. The news comes as Sprouts continues to expand its presence in the region.

feed.title: How to make Pesto Alla Trapanese, a recipe from the chefs at Cicala Restaurant using Portofino Italian-style tuna
feed.summary: Chef/owners Joe & Angela Cicala of Cicala Restaurant have partnered with Portofino Italian-style tuna to share a recipe for Pesto Alla Trapanese. This simple pasta dish promises to transport diners to Sicily, featuring ingredients like olive oil, cherry tomatoes, basil, pecorino, and Portofino tuna. The article also mentions a sweepstakes for Portofino products and a Cicala gift card.

feed.title: Liberty Media CEO Sees Amazon Driving Big Tech’s TV Sports Rights Ambitions
feed.summary: Liberty Media CEO Greg Maffei believes Amazon is leading the charge for Big Tech platforms to acquire more international sports rights, driven by their increasing involvement in agreements like NFL games. Maffei highlighted the growing seriousness of Amazon's investments in sports content and predicted other tech giants would follow suit. He also noted that the break up of the cable bundle and pressure on regional sports networks are contributing factors to Silicon Valley's pursuit of global sports rights.

feed.title: Amazon spends $131 million for stake in cargo airline ATSG
feed.summary: Amazon acquired a minority stake in Air Transport Services Group Inc. (ATSG) for $131 million, signaling its intention to expand its air-cargo operations. This purchase involved exercising previously acquired warrants and is contingent on U.S. Department of Transportation approval. The move further solidifies Amazon's efforts to reduce reliance on third-party logistics partners by growing its own air freight network.

feed.title: IonQ To Become The First Publicly Traded Pure-Play Quantum Computing Company
