feed.summary: IonQ announced its definitive merger agreement with dMY Technology Group III (NYSE: DMYI.U), which will result in IonQ becoming the first publicly traded pure-play quantum computing company on the NYSE under the symbol “IONQ”. The transaction is expected to generate $650 million in gross proceeds, including a $350 million PIPE investment from major institutional and strategic investors, leading to a pro forma implied market capitalization of approximately $2 billion. IonQ aims to leverage this capital to advance its mission of solving critical societal problems through quantum computing, with plans to develop networked modular quantum computers by 2023.

feed.title: Tesla loses a third of its value for the third time in a year
feed.summary: Tesla Inc.'s stock experienced its third significant drop in a year, losing a third of its value from its January record high, primarily due to investor concerns about rising interest rates affecting high-valuation stocks. The selloff wiped off nearly $300 billion from its market capitalization, moving it behind Facebook. This decline is more pronounced than other major tech companies, and its shares have steadily fallen since announcing a bitcoin purchase, even as bitcoin’s price climbed.

feed.title: Observability moves to the center of the distributed systems conversation
feed.summary: In modern distributed systems, the concept of "monitoring" has evolved into "observability" to provide a deeper understanding of system health beyond just performance metrics. Observability tools leverage telemetry data, including metrics, logs, and traces, to determine not just what is wrong but why, offering an end-to-end user experience view. Key players like AWS, Oracle, and Splunk are enhancing their observability platforms with AI and machine learning to address the complexities of cloud-native architectures and support DevOps teams.

feed.title: Report: Seresto Flea and Tick Collars Linked To Pet Illnesses, Deaths, Human Side Effects
feed.summary: A report by USA Today and the Midwest Center for Investigative Reporting has linked Seresto flea and tick collars to nearly 1,700 pet deaths and 75,000 incident reports, including side effects in almost 1,000 humans. The investigation, based on EPA documents, suggests the agency has been aware of these issues since 2012 but has not adequately informed the public. Both the EPA and Elanco Animal Health, the current seller of the collars, affirm the product's safety while Amazon is reviewing its sale.

feed.title: Kroger brings 80 Acres Farms vertically farmed produce to more stores
feed.summary: Kroger is expanding its partnership with 80 Acres Farms to offer vertically farmed produce in over 300 stores across Ohio, Indiana, and Kentucky, as well as online. This expansion follows a successful 15-month pilot and aligns with Kroger’s Zero Hunger | Zero Waste initiative. Other grocers, like Albertsons, are also increasing their offerings of vertically farmed produce, highlighting a growing trend in sustainable agriculture and fresh food accessibility.

feed.title: Tesla shares bounce back following steep five-day decline
feed.summary: Tesla shares rebounded on Tuesday after experiencing a significant five-day decline, which erased about $149 billion from its valuation. The recovery was fueled by positive news, including an analyst upgrade from New Street Research, a rally in Bitcoin, and a general shift in sentiment towards high-multiple technology stocks. Analyst Pierre Ferragu upgraded Tesla to buy, citing strong earnings momentum and demand, suggesting the stock could trade at a high multiple similar to Amazon's historical performance.

feed.title: The Sonos Roam is a small, portable smart speaker for $169
feed.summary: Sonos has unveiled its smallest and most portable smart speaker to date, the Sonos Roam, priced at $169. This lightweight device, weighing under a pound, is designed for on-the-go use and features an IP67 rating for water and dust resistance, as well as Qi wireless charging compatibility. It boasts enhanced sound quality with Automatic TruePlay Tuning, connects via Wi-Fi and Bluetooth, and supports over 100 streaming services and multiple voice assistants.

feed.title: WWE® SuperCard Celebrates 25 Years of “Stone Cold” Steve Austin with New Content Available Now
feed.summary: 2K has announced new content for WWE SuperCard to celebrate the 25th anniversary of "Stone Cold" Steve Austin's WWE debut. Throughout March, players can enjoy new items and events, including a "Stone Cold" Steve Austin cardback, his iconic jean shorts, and a "Stone Cold" Fusion event starting March 9th to create Special Edition cards.

feed.title: Going small to go big: Burlington Stores shrinks store size to add another 1,000 more locations
feed.summary: Burlington Stores Inc. plans to significantly expand its physical retail footprint by adding another 1,000 locations. This expansion strategy involves shrinking store sizes, which is expected to lead to lower occupancy costs and higher operating margins by requiring less in-store inventory. The company is pursuing this aggressive growth while many other retailers are contracting.

feed.title: FedEx completes shift of former Postal Service business, rebrands service
feed.summary: FedEx has completed the integration of its last-mile delivery operations, formerly handled by the U.S. Postal Service, and rebranded the service to FedEx Ground Economy from FedEx SmartPost. This shift aims to build package density in its business-to-consumer ground network and enable seven-day-a-week deliveries. The transition was delayed from its original 2020 target due to the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic on parcel deliveries.

feed.title: ServiceNow Customer Service Management grows in Quebec
feed.summary: ServiceNow has released its twice-yearly platform update, named Quebec, with new workflow automation features for Customer Service Management users. The update includes mobile messaging support, a new Engagement Messenger for self-service actions, and Process Optimization for analyzing customer service processes. These enhancements aim to expand ServiceNow's presence in the customer service and contact center market, targeting growth in areas like telecommunications and financial services, building on previous features introduced during the pandemic.

feed.title: UNFI branches out into bitcoin and a local marketplace
feed.summary: United Natural Foods, Inc. (UNFI) is expanding its services by launching a local products marketplace for retailers and partnering with Coin Cloud to place bitcoin kiosks in grocery stores. The company reported strong Q2 earnings with adjusted earnings up 57% and net sales up 7.1%, driven by increased at-home consumption and successful cross-selling efforts. UNFI also extended its supply contract with Whole Foods until 2027 and expects to see significant inflation and promotional activity increase in the coming fiscal year.

feed.title: A New Outdoor Gear Marketplace Launches With The Backing Of VF Corporation
feed.summary: Out&Back Outdoor Inc., a new Denver-based e-commerce startup, has launched an online secondary marketplace for outdoor gear with an early investment from VF Corporation. The platform aims to revolutionize how consumers buy and sell outdoor apparel by offering a "Kayak for outdoor gear" experience, allowing users to compare new and used items from various brands on a single site. Out&Back plans to generate revenue through affiliate sales and by buying, cleaning, and reselling used gear, promoting sustainability within the outdoor industry.

feed.title: Industrial ETFs Like 'DUSL' Riding a Wave of Market Momentum
feed.summary: The article discusses the recent market shift towards industrial stocks, citing the Direxion Daily Industrials Bull 3X Shares (DUSL) as a potential investment for leveraged gains. It highlights how industrials are performing well as the economy strengthens, attracting capital away from technology stocks. The SPDR Industrial ETF (XLI) has reached a new high, contrasting with the SPDR Technology ETF (XLK)'s decline, indicating a move towards value stocks benefiting from economic growth and higher interest rates.

feed.title: 4 Key Steps to Profitable Store-Based Fulfillment
feed.summary: A report by Incisiv, commissioned by Manhattan Associates Inc., reveals that while ecommerce surged due to the pandemic, order fulfillment from stores has significantly reduced profit margins for retailers. The article outlines four key steps for retailers to make store-based fulfillment more profitable: addressing inefficiencies, equipping associates with digital tools, streamlining returns, and improving in-store inventory accuracy. These steps are crucial for retailers to adapt to the evolving landscape and meet customer expectations in a post-pandemic world.

feed.title: GM CEO Mary Barra: 'We're just at the beginning' of where company's stock will move
feed.summary: GM CEO Mary Barra believes the company's stock is poised for significant growth as the automaker shifts its focus to all-electric vehicles (EVs). Shares of GM are up 36% this year, and Barra anticipates this upward trend will continue as the market recognizes GM's assets and resources in this transformation. She highlighted GM's new EV commercial business, BrightDrop, as an example of innovation beyond just changing the propulsion system.

feed.title: Target, The 'Apple Experience' And Training
feed.summary: This article discusses Target's strategy for staffing its new in-store Apple kiosks with specially trained employees, emphasizing the importance of knowledgeable staff for customer satisfaction and sales. It highlights how co-branding extends to employee training, crucial for retail partnerships and reducing employee turnover, while warning against exclusive training that could undermine collaborative culture.

feed.title: PepsiCo subsidiary expands keto snack brand
feed.summary: Hilo Life, a keto-friendly snack brand owned by PepsiCo's subsidiary Inviting Foods LLC, is launching new cheese puffs in cheddar and fiery hot flavors. These new products, along with their existing tortilla-style chips and snack mixes, aim to satisfy consumers looking for low-carb, flavorful snack options. The snack puffs contain 3 grams of net carbohydrates and 12 grams of protein per serving.

feed.title: Amazon Announces First Fulfillment Center in Amarillo, TX
feed.summary: Amazon.com, Inc. (NASDAQ: AMZN) plans to open its first fulfillment center in Amarillo, Texas, creating over 500 new, full-time jobs. This one million square-foot facility, expected to launch in early 2022, will handle bulky customer items and provide comprehensive benefits and competitive wages to employees. Local and state leaders, including Amarillo Mayor Ginger Nelson, lauded the investment for its significant economic boost to the Texas Panhandle.

feed.title: Subscription E-Commerce Market: 3 Bold Projections for 2021 |
feed.summary: HTF Market Intelligence Consulting Pvt. Ltd. has released a detailed research study on the Global Subscription E-Commerce Market, offering a product outlook and market review until 2026. The report segments the market by regions, types (Beauty and Personal Care, Food and Beverage, Clothing and Fashion, Entertainment & Health and Fitness), and applications (Online & Offline), highlighting key players such as Dollar Shave Club, Netflix, and Hello Fresh. It provides competitive analysis, financial ratios, and strategic recommendations to help businesses understand market dynamics and identify growth opportunities.

feed.title: Transformers Kingdom Blackarachnia Concept Art by Ken Christiansen
feed.summary: Ken Christiansen has released new concept art for the Transformers Kingdom Blackarachnia figure. The artwork, commissioned in partnership with Hasbro, Inc., showcases the "Black Arachnia Bot Mode" and is available on Hasbro Pulse. The article includes several images of the concept art and provides links to purchase the figure from various online retailers.
feed.banner_image: https://i.tformers.com/g/albums/40688/Transformers Kingdom Blackarachnia Concept Art by Ken Christiansen (1).jpg

feed.title: Coupang soars 41% on US trading debut
feed.summary: Coupang's shares soared 41% on its US trading debut, closing at $49.25 after raising $4.6 billion in its IPO. The company is now South Korea's third-largest by market value and plans to invest in logistics and technology to enhance its competitive edge against domestic rivals. Analysts note its growth potential despite being a loss-making entity, comparing its data-driven approach to Amazon.

feed.title: Coty’s Jean-Denis Mariani Discusses Digital Transformation
feed.summary: Jean-Denis Mariani, Coty's chief digital officer, discusses the company's digital transformation, emphasizing that "there is no digital strategy anymore. It’s just strategy in a digital world.” He highlights the importance of content, omnichannel investment, and localization, particularly in China. Mariani also shares insights from Kylie Skin and KKW Beauty, including the power of diverse content, the "drop" model, and community building, and predicts video will eclipse photographs online.

feed.title: The New Possible Podcast: Vortic Watch Co: Not Your Grandpa’s Pocket Watch
feed.summary: This article introduces an episode of The New Possible podcast featuring Vortic Watch Co., a company that repurposes WWII-era American pocket watches into modern wristwatches. Founders RT Custer and Tyler Wolfe discuss their business model, which involves restoring old pocket watch mechanisms and manufacturing new cases and components using Autodesk Fusion 360, focusing on transparent and U.S.-based production. The podcast highlights their journey, including pivoting ideas, crowdfunding success, and bringing manufacturing in-house.

feed.title: Score 50% Off Your Fave Pizza With These Pi Day Deals
feed.summary: This article details numerous Pi Day (March 14) deals for 2021, offering discounts on pizzas and pies from various national retailers. Deals include $3.14 pizzas from 7-Eleven and Blaze Pizza, 50% off select pizzas at BJ's Restaurant & Brewhouse, and discounts through apps like Slice and Uber Eats. Customers are encouraged to follow CDC guidelines for social distancing and consider takeout or delivery.

feed.title: Lands' End credits Kohl's partnership for growing customer base
feed.summary: Lands' End is expanding its customer base by utilizing multiple sales channels, including partnerships with retailers like Kohl's and online platforms such as Amazon. This strategy is helping the company reach a broader audience, according to its top executive. The article highlights that these partnerships are crucial for the brand's growth and increased market penetration.

feed.title: Buffer inventory, e-commerce growth buoys warehouse demand: Prologis
feed.summary: A report from Prologis indicates that increased focus on supply chain resilience and the continued growth of e-commerce are expected to significantly boost demand for logistics real estate. An estimated 125 million square feet or more of new warehousing space will be needed annually in the U.S. and Europe until 2025 to meet e-commerce demand. Furthermore, a shift away from just-in-time practices towards higher inventories for resiliency could add another 57 million to 114 million square feet in demand over the next five years.

feed.title: Adobe Digital Economy Index: COVID-19 Report
feed.summary: The Adobe Digital Economy Index report analyzes the lasting impact of COVID-19 on eCommerce, revealing an extra boost of $183 billion to online spending and projecting 2022 to be the first trillion-dollar year for eCommerce. It highlights significant changes in consumer behavior, such as the boom in "Buy Now, Pay Later" options, surging online grocery sales, and persistent out-of-stock messages. The report also notes a slump in digital purchasing power and atypical pricing trends, with digital inflation observed across major online categories.

feed.title: AMD Unveils EPYC 'Milan' 7003 CPUs, Zen 3 Comes to 64-Core Server Chips
feed.summary: AMD has officially launched its EPYC 'Milan' 7003 processors, bringing the Zen 3 architecture to the server market with its flagship 64-core EPYC 7763, claiming it to be the world's fastest server processor. These new 7nm chips are drop-in compatible with existing servers and offer significant performance improvements over Intel's Xeon Cascade Lake Refresh chips, particularly in HPC, Cloud, and enterprise workloads, alongside a better price-to-performance ratio. The Milan processors also focus on expanding AMD's hardware and software ecosystems to better cater to a broader range of enterprise customers.

feed.title: GM-backed Cruise to buy self-driving start-up Voyage
feed.summary: GM's majority-owned autonomous vehicle subsidiary Cruise has announced its agreement to acquire self-driving car start-up Voyage. Voyage CEO Oliver Cameron will join Cruise as vice president of product, and most of Voyage's 60 employees are expected to transition to Cruise. This acquisition marks a continued consolidation within the autonomous vehicle sector as Cruise focuses on launching a robotaxi business in San Francisco.

feed.title: AMD Raises Bar Against Intel With 3rd-Gen EPYC Milan CPUS
feed.summary: AMD has launched its third-generation EPYC processors, codenamed Milan, aiming to surpass Intel in performance and total cost of ownership for data centers. The new processors offer significant performance improvements, enhanced security features, and backward compatibility with existing server platforms, providing a competitive edge over Intel's upcoming Xeon Scalable chips. AMD emphasizes the value proposition of Milan through its high core density, strong price-performance ratio, and reduced TCO for cloud, enterprise, and HPC workloads.

feed.title: Sources: Amazon Web Services will keep more than 500 employees in East Palo Alto despite shedding office space there
feed.summary: Amazon Web Services (AWS) plans to retain over 500 employees in East Palo Alto, despite its impending departure from a significant Class A office property in the area. The company, a subsidiary of Amazon.com Inc., will continue to maintain a substantial presence in the mid-Peninsula city. This decision comes even as AWS sheds some of its office space, indicating a strategic shift rather than a complete withdrawal from the location.

feed.title: Fidelity Magellan ETF ETF Price, Holdings, & News (BATS:FMAG)
feed.summary: The Fidelity Magellan ETF (FMAG) is an actively managed, non-transparent ETF that invests in fundamentally selected firms globally, tracking the S&P 500 TR USD index. Launched in February 2021, the fund has assets under management of $266.85 million and a net expense ratio of 0.59%. Its top holdings include major tech companies like NVIDIA, Microsoft, and Amazon.com.

feed.title: For Sun Country's IPO, investors make airline worth $2 billion
feed.summary: Sun Country Airlines' initial public offering saw its stock surge over 50% on its first day of trading, indicating strong investor confidence in the airline and the recovery of air travel. The $218.2 million raised will be used to pay off a federal loan, acquire new airplanes, and hire more staff. The IPO values the company at $2.03 billion, providing a significant return for Apollo Global Management, which acquired Sun Country in 2018.

feed.title: Amazon unseats Walmart to become the No. 1 apparel retailer in U.S., Wells Fargo says
feed.summary: Amazon has surpassed Walmart to become the top apparel retailer in the U.S., driven largely by a pandemic-fueled e-commerce boom, according to Wells Fargo. The research indicates Amazon's U.S. apparel and footwear sales grew by about 15% in 2020 to over $41 billion, significantly outperforming Walmart. This gives Amazon an estimated 11-12% share of all U.S. apparel sales and 34-35% of all online apparel sales.

feed.title: Amazon’s New Grocery Store Chain Is More Traditional Than It Looks
feed.summary: Amazon's new grocery store chain appears more traditional than expected, as the company enters the $900 billion U.S. grocery market which has historically been a digital laggard. Despite the challenges of online grocery delivery, the pandemic has accelerated digital adoption, with Instacart and Uber expanding into the sector, and established retailers like Kroger improving their online offerings.

feed.title: The Big Reason Why Angie’s List is Changing Its Name
feed.summary: Angie's List has rebranded as Angi Inc. to better reflect its expanded services beyond just a list of contractors, including other housing-related businesses like HomeAdvisor and Handy. The change aims to move past the literal interpretation of "Angie's List" as the company now facilitates finding, booking, and paying contractors, a demand that has increased during the COVID-19 pandemic. Angi Inc. continues to succeed despite competition from major players like Amazon.

feed.title: Here's how much Wheels Up paid for each of its 5 acquisitions
feed.summary: Wheels Up spent less than $100 million in cash to acquire five companies, building itself into the second-largest private jet operator with a $2 billion valuation ahead of its public offering via SPAC. The article details the cash, stock, and other considerations for each acquisition: Travel Management Company, Avianis Systems, Delta Private Jets, Gama Aviation, and Mountain Aviation. It reveals the financial breakdown of these strategic moves that transformed Wheels Up from a marketing organization into a major operator.

feed.title: At $2.08 Trillion, Apple Is Bigger Than These Things
feed.summary: As of March 15, 2021, Apple Inc. (AAPL) boasts a market capitalization of $2.08 trillion, making it the world's largest public company. This staggering figure surpasses the GDP of most countries, the total market value of many global stock exchanges, the U.S. budget deficit, and the inflation-adjusted costs of multiple major U.S. wars. It also exceeds the combined net worth of the world's 18 richest people.

feed.title: Sources: Amazon headed to Bryton Commerce Center in Huntersville for more industrial space
feed.summary: Amazon is reportedly expanding its industrial footprint in Huntersville, North Carolina, by targeting Building C at Bryton Commerce Center, a 125,937-square-foot industrial building. City documents confirm this move, and sources suggest Amazon is also looking at a neighboring vacant site for additional space. This expansion reflects the e-commerce giant's rapid growth in the Charlotte area.

feed.title: Imperva Snags Akamai’s Micheal McCollough To Lead Channels
feed.summary: Imperva has hired Micheal McCollough, formerly Akamai's global channel chief, to lead and expand its channel business, with a focus on becoming a channel-first company. McCollough aims to strengthen relationships with public cloud providers, enhance services for existing partners, and leverage the company's acquisition of jSonar. He plans to incentivize partners through new programs, potentially driving almost all new business through the channel and increasing their involvement in services and support.

feed.title: Space junk from satellites puts global networks at risk
feed.summary: The increasing number of satellites in Low Earth Orbit (LEO) is creating a significant risk of collisions and space debris, threatening global communication networks. The article highlights past incidents, the challenges of tracking satellites, and the "Kessler Syndrome," where collisions could create cascading debris fields. While some private companies are developing mitigation strategies, there is currently no binding international framework to address this growing problem.

feed.title: New e-commerce warehouse coming to Oshawa
feed.summary: Canadian e-commerce company Aosom is establishing a new warehouse operation in Oshawa's south end, occupying 170,150 sq. ft. in an industrial development by Panattoni Development Company. This move is expected to create new jobs and stimulate economic growth, reinforcing Oshawa's strong industrial sector and low vacancy rates. The warehouse is projected to open in September, marking Panattoni's first lease at the new Thornton Rd. S. facility.

feed.title: Google debuts A2 VMs powered by Nvidia A100 GPUs, its largest cloud instances yet
feed.summary: Google has announced the general availability of its new Compute Engine A2 virtual machines, powered by Nvidia's Ampere A100 Tensor Core GPUs. These A2 VMs are notable for being the first in the world to integrate up to 16 Nvidia A100 GPUs into a single instance, making them the most powerful single-node GPU offering from any public cloud provider. Designed for demanding machine learning and high-performance computing workloads, Google claims they offer significant performance improvements and cost efficiencies compared to previous generations.

feed.title: QYLG Stock Price | Global X Nasdaq 100 Covered Call & Growth ETF
feed.summary: This article provides comprehensive information about the Global X Nasdaq 100 Covered Call & Growth ETF (QYLG), including its current stock price, trading exchange (NASDAQ), and key financial metrics like dividend yield, expense ratio, and market capitalization. It also features technical analysis indicators and a list of its top holdings, offering a detailed overview for potential investors.

feed.title: Les Wexner exits board, severs last ties to retail empire
feed.summary: Les Wexner, the billionaire founder of L Brands, is severing his last ties with the retail empire by not seeking re-election to the company's board of directors. This decision follows his earlier step down as CEO and chairman and comes as L Brands plans to separate its Victoria's Secret and Bath & Body Works businesses. Wexner's reputation was also affected by his association with the late Jeffrey Epstein.

feed.title: Roblox stock: meteoric growth, but what happens when the lockdowns end?
feed.summary: Roblox Corporation experienced significant user and revenue growth in 2020 due to pandemic lockdowns, leading to a successful direct listing on the NYSE. While analysts predict continued strong performance, the article discusses potential hurdles such as the return to normalcy post-pandemic, unsophisticated graphics, platform bugs, and safety concerns, alongside strategies for future growth such as expanding to teenage demographics and international markets.

feed.title: XPO Logistics Unveils GXO as New Company Name for Planned Spin-off of Logistics Business
feed.summary: XPO Logistics announced that GXO Logistics, Inc. will be the new company name for its planned spin-off of its logistics business. GXO, standing for "game-changing opportunities," aims to capitalize on e-commerce growth, automation, and outsourcing. XPO has filed a confidential initial Form 10 registration statement with the SEC, with the transaction expected in the second half of 2021.

feed.title: Zoom Pays $0 in Federal Income Taxes on Pandemic Profits
feed.summary: Zoom Video Communications, despite seeing its profits increase by over 4,000 percent in 2020, paid no federal corporate income tax. This tax avoidance, largely attributed to the lavish use of executive stock options and other tax breaks like accelerated depreciation and R&D credits, mirrors strategies used by other tech giants. The article highlights this as an example of corporate tax avoidance that policymakers should address in future tax reform.
feed.source: Institute on Taxation and Economic Policy
feed.source_domain: Institute on Taxation and Economic Policy

feed.title: How Etsy Became the One-Stop Shop for ‘Mama Needs Wine’ Merch
feed.summary: This article explores how the phrase "Mama Needs Wine" became widely popular on Etsy, transforming the platform into a primary source for related merchandise. It highlights the role of early sellers like Tori Tinnon of Mama Bird & Co. and Melissa Horvath of Sweet Water Decor in commercializing the meme, and how the trend has been further boosted by the COVID-19 pandemic. The piece explains that the success lies in the simple, relatable truth of the statement for mothers, despite challenges like copycat sellers.

feed.title: Google Cloud Pricing vs AWS: A Fair Comparison?
feed.summary: This article provides a detailed comparison of Google Cloud Platform (GCP) and Amazon Web Services (AWS) pricing for compute instances and storage. It highlights differences in market positioning, on-demand and reserved VM pricing, and various storage options, noting where direct comparisons can be misleading due to differing structures. The article also discusses cost optimization strategies using NetApp Cloud Volumes ONTAP.

feed.title: WUGI Stock Price | Esoterica NextG Economy ETF
feed.summary: This article provides current and historical financial data for the Esoterica NextG Economy ETF (WUGI), including its current stock price, trading ranges, dividends, and key statistics like expense ratio and market cap. It also details the ETF's holdings, with major positions in companies like Taiwan Semiconductor, NVIDIA, and Broadcom. The fund's profile highlights its investment strategy across communication services and information technology sectors.

feed.title: These 10 retailers are in expansion mode: Here's who is opening new stores
feed.summary: In a significant shift from 2020, retailers in the U.S. are announcing more store openings than closures so far this year, with 3,199 openings compared to 2,548 closures. This trend is driven by a rebound in consumer spending expectations, the availability of more favorable real estate terms, and a recovering economy post-pandemic. Several major retailers, including Ulta Beauty, Sephora, Burlington Stores, Amazon, and Dick's Sporting Goods, are planning substantial expansions, often focusing on smaller formats, off-mall locations, or strategic partnerships to reach customers more effectively.

feed.title: Scammers trick Android users into installing fake, malware-laden Clubhouse app
feed.summary: Scammers are exploiting the demand for the audio-based social app Clubhouse among Android users by distributing a fake, malware-laden version of the app. This fraudulent Android app, discovered by ESET researchers, contains a Trojan virus called "BlackRock" that can steal login information from over 458 online services, including financial apps, social media, and cryptocurrency exchanges, and can also intercept text messages, bypassing two-factor authentication. Experts warn that the absence of an official Android version of Clubhouse creates a significant vulnerability that cybercriminals are eager to exploit, making an official Android app the ultimate solution.

feed.title: EXCLUSIVE Low tips, long waits: DoorDash takes on drivers’ Walmart gripes
feed.summary: DoorDash is addressing frustrations from its independent drivers regarding Walmart deliveries, specifically concerning low customer tips and long wait times at stores. The company surveyed drivers about their experiences and made internal changes to its app, while also working with Walmart to enable upfront tipping in Walmart's own app, which has resulted in 75% of shoppers allocating tips. This move comes as DoorDash expands its delivery services beyond urban restaurants to compete with other e-commerce giants.

feed.title: Carrier Offers Solutions to Meet Increased COVID-19 Vaccine Distribution Demand
feed.summary: Carrier Global Corporation is offering solutions to support the increased demand for COVID-19 vaccine distribution in the U.S., including transport refrigeration, mobile cold storage, and temperature monitoring systems. These solutions, integrated with its Lynx™ digital platform, are designed to help safely move, track, and store hundreds of millions of vaccine doses. Carrier is working with various partners across the healthcare supply chain to ensure vaccine efficacy and accessibility for mass vaccination efforts.

feed.title: Chart Industries plans Alabama growth project, creating 94 jobs
feed.summary: Chart Industries, a Georgia-based equipment maker for clean energy and industrial gas markets, plans a $2.5 million expansion in Theodore, Alabama. This project will create 94 new jobs and significantly increase the facility's capacity for hydrogen transport trailers and introduce hydrogen bulk storage tank production. The expansion highlights the company's confidence in Mobile's workforce and logistical assets.

feed.title: Intel to Spend $20 Billion on 2 New Chip Factories in Arizona. (Published 2021)
feed.summary: Intel's new CEO, Patrick Gelsinger, announced plans to invest $20 billion in two new chip factories in Arizona. This move signifies Intel's commitment to doubling down on chip manufacturing in the U.S. and Europe, aiming to become a major manufacturer for other companies, addressing concerns about component shortages and reliance on Asian factories. The initiative also seeks to regain Intel's footing in the competitive chip production market, where it had previously fallen behind competitors like TSMC and Samsung.

feed.title: GameStop plunges, but Reddit followers tout stock once again as e-commerce sales surge
feed.summary: GameStop's shares plummeted over 10% after reporting mixed earnings, yet Reddit's WallStreetBets community continued to rally behind the stock, emphasizing strong year-to-date performance and surging e-commerce sales. The company announced former Amazon and Google executive Jenna Owens as its new COO, underscoring its commitment to a digital transformation led by Chewy founder Ryan Cohen. Despite missing some Wall Street expectations, GameStop continues to strategically strengthen its executive team and explore initiatives to fund future growth amid its pivot to e-commerce.

feed.title: Garmin announces integration of Amazon’s Alexa Custom
feed.summary: Garmin International, Inc. has expanded its strategic relationship with Amazon by integrating Alexa Custom Assistant into its in-vehicle infotainment systems. This solution allows automakers to create their own intelligent branded assistants tailored to their brand personality and customer needs, leveraging Alexa's advanced AI. Garmin is the first Tier 1 auto OEM supplier to integrate these capabilities, further solidifying its collaboration with Amazon in offering cutting-edge voice solutions to leading automotive manufacturers.

feed.title: Sonos drops the needle on high-resolution music streaming via Qobuz
feed.summary: Sonos has announced that Qobuz will be the first streaming music service to deliver 24-bit resolution audio to its platform, available through the Sonos S2 app. While Sonos enabled high-resolution audio last fall for local files, this marks the first time a streaming service will provide 24-bit audio to Sonos speakers. Qobuz offers unlimited 24-bit streaming up to 192kHz, but Sonos speakers will stream at a maximum of 24-bit/48kHz, with older S2-compatible devices supporting 16-bit.

feed.title: FDA Expands Indication of Use for Tivic Health's ClearUP Sinus Pain Relief to Include Congestion for Common Cold, Flu and Allergies
feed.summary: Tivic Health announced that the FDA has expanded the indication for use of its ClearUP Sinus Pain Relief device to include congestion from allergies, the flu, and the common cold. This expansion builds on its existing FDA clearance for allergy-related sinus pain. Clinical studies showed significant reduction in congestion symptoms, positioning ClearUP as a drug-free alternative for sinus and nasal congestion relief.

feed.title: Tech Moves: Startup Haven grows team to lead new fund; PSL promotions; F5 Networks adds execs
feed.summary: This article details significant "Tech Moves" in the Pacific Northwest, including Startup Haven launching a new investment fund and expanding its team. Pioneer Square Labs (PSL) announced three leadership promotions, and F5 Networks hired new executives and promoted an existing one. Additionally, various other Seattle-area companies like Redfin, Fabric, Impinj, Strike Graph, Reverb, and Curi Bio made key hires, promotions, or board appointments.

feed.title: Intel to spend $20 billion on U.S. chip plants as CEO challenges Asia dominance
feed.summary: Intel announced plans to invest $20 billion in two new chip manufacturing plants in Arizona, aiming to expand its advanced chip production and open its factories to outside customers. This move by CEO Pat Gelsinger directly challenges the dominance of TSMC and Samsung in the advanced chip market and seeks to shift the technological balance of power back to the U.S. and Europe. The company expects $72 billion in revenue and adjusted earnings per share of $4.55 for 2021, and the new strategy is intended to restore Intel's reputation and create 3,000 permanent jobs.

feed.title: Old Golf Courses Are Being Turned Into E-Commerce Warehouses
feed.summary: Driven by a surge in online shopping and the need for more e-commerce fulfillment centers, developers are acquiring old golf courses and converting them into warehouses. This trend is fueled by the overbuilding of golf courses in the early 2000s and a subsequent decline in their popularity, making these large undeveloped tracts of land ideal for logistics conversion.

feed.title: Independent cloud provider DigitalOcean drops in Wall Street debut
feed.summary: DigitalOcean, a small-scale cloud infrastructure provider, debuted on the New York Stock Exchange under the ticker symbol "DOCN" with shares opening lower than their IPO price. The company raised $775 million in its IPO and aims to challenge larger cloud providers like Amazon and Microsoft by focusing on ease of use and personalized support for small and medium-sized businesses. Despite a net loss in 2020, DigitalOcean plans to increase operating margins and expand its data center footprint.

feed.title: Hasbro Unveils New ‘Baby Yoda’ Grogu Animatronic
feed.summary: Hasbro has launched a new "Star Wars Galactic Snackin' Grogu" animatronic, an updated version of their popular "Baby Yoda" toy from The Mandalorian. This animatronic features sounds, animated motions, interactive accessories that trigger eating sounds, and Force activation inspired by the Disney+ series. It is currently available for pre-order at major retailers.

feed.title: Amazon Washington Delivery Drivers $8.2M Class Action Settlement
feed.summary: Amazon and several delivery service providers have agreed to an $8.2 million class action settlement for Washington delivery drivers. The lawsuit alleged violations of wage and hour laws and Seattle's municipal code. Eligible class members, who drove for specific providers between December 2014 and July 2020, will receive a proportional share of the settlement fund.

feed.title: 9 Great eCommerce Promotion Ideas to Boost Conversions
feed.summary: This article outlines nine effective eCommerce promotion ideas to help businesses boost conversions and retain customers. It covers strategies such as free shipping, various discount types (percentage-based, multi-purchase, quantity), flash sales, limited-time coupons, contests, free samples, and giveaways. The article emphasizes combining these promotions with other marketing strategies for maximum impact.

feed.title: Hackers breach SalusCare patient and employee records; nearly 86,000 files at risk
feed.summary: SalusCare, a major mental health service provider in Southwest Florida, reported a data breach affecting up to 85,688 patient and employee records following a malware attack. The hackers gained access via a phishing scam and downloaded the organization's entire database to an Amazon cloud storage account. SalusCare has filed a lawsuit to compel Amazon to provide audit logs to determine which data was accessed and has since secured access to the compromised account.

feed.title: 20 Million Miners: Finding Malicious Cryptojacking Images in Docker Hub
feed.summary: This article details the discovery of 30 malicious cryptojacking container images on Docker Hub, which accumulated over 20 million pulls and generated an estimated $200,000 for attackers. The research highlights the prevalence of Monero mining due to its anonymity and CPU-friendliness, predominantly using the XMRig cryptominer. It also emphasizes the challenges of detecting such threats in cloud environments and how Palo Alto Networks products protect against them.

feed.title: Amazon in talks to expand its Amazon Fresh grocery concept to St. Louis
feed.summary: Amazon is reportedly in discussions to open an Amazon Fresh grocery store in Sunset Hills, St. Louis, which would mark its first location in Missouri. The proposed store would occupy a vacant Toys "R" Us storefront at the Shoppes at Sunset Hills. The commercial real estate firm Sansone Group is involved in the redevelopment and is seeking to establish a community improvement district to fund a portion of the costs, although they have not publicly named Amazon as the tenant.

feed.title: Numbers of Etsy sellers and buyers skyrocket during pandemic - Buffalo Business First
feed.summary: The COVID-19 pandemic led to a significant increase in the number of sellers and buyers on e-commerce platforms like Etsy. With events canceled and people spending more time at home, many turned to online marketplaces. This surge in activity on Etsy is highlighted in a Buffalo Business First article.

feed.title: This CBD Drink Keeps Jessica Alba Centered
feed.summary: Jessica Alba, founder of The Honest Company, shared her self-care routine, emphasizing the use of high-quality, non-toxic products. Her regimen includes a CBD drink for winding down, an Honest Beauty mask, an Oura Ring for sleep tracking, and a matcha smoothie recipe. Alba also highlighted her preferred Honest Beauty products for outer glow and hydration.

feed.title: Coca-Cola Research Finds Five Major Pandemic-Era Consumer Trends
feed.summary: Coca-Cola's "Refreshing Insights" report identifies five key consumer trends shaped by the pandemic: a heightened focus on health, an increase in at-home activities, a greater desire for comfort and indulgence, accelerated e-commerce adoption, and a redefinition of value. These trends highlight lasting shifts in consumer behavior regarding purchasing, daily routines, emotional needs, and spending priorities.

feed.title: Integrating with Telephone Networks to Enable Real-Time AI Services
feed.summary: Ribbon Communications, in collaboration with NVIDIA, developed a solution to analyze contact center calls in real-time using advanced AI. The Ribbon AI gateway acts as a secure bridge converting Real-time Transport Protocol (RTP) audio streams into an AI-acceptable audio format for transcription and analysis. This architecture allows businesses to dynamically control call handling using REST APIs for applications like regulatory compliance, fraud analysis, and sentiment analysis, addressing critical limitations of delayed call recording analysis.

feed.title: What sold, and for how much, in Ocala/Marion County
feed.summary: This article lists warranty deed transfers in Ocala/Marion County, Florida, for prices exceeding $60,000, recorded from February 22-26, 2021. It details numerous property sales, including the names of sellers, buyers, and the sale prices across various subdivisions and estates within the county. Some noteworthy transactions include properties in Golden Ocala and Jumbolair Aviation Estates with high sale values.

feed.title: One taste is all it takes with new Blue Buffalo cat food
feed.summary: Blue Buffalo has launched "Tastefuls," a new line of wet cat food designed to appeal to even the pickiest felines. The products adhere to the "True BLUE Promise" using natural ingredients, real meat as the first ingredient, and no by-products or artificial additives. The launch is supported by an integrated marketing campaign, "One Taste Is All It Takes," featuring ESPN host Nicole Briscoe.

feed.title: JBG SMITH Starts Residential Construction at Amazon HQ2
feed.summary: JBG SMITH has commenced construction on two residential towers at 1900 Crystal Drive in National Landing, Arlington, near Amazon's HQ2. This mixed-use development will feature 808 rental units and 40,000 square feet of retail space. The project is a significant milestone in the transformation of National Landing, with JBG SMITH also involved in Amazon's Housing Equity Fund for affordable units in the area.
